user_message: Generate a South Indian Recipes recipe for Brahmi Chutney Recipe - Brahmi Chutney (Recipe In Hindi)
reference_text:
**Brahmi Chutney Recipe - Brahmi Chutney (Recipe In Hindi)**


**Ingredients:**
2 teaspoons coconut oil, salt - as per taste,1 cup Brahmi leaves, 1/2 cup coconut - tightly Tamarind - a little

**Instructions:**
To make Brahmi chutney, first clean and wash the Brahmi leaves.
Now heat coconut oil in a pan.
Now add urad dal, dry red chillies, curry leaves and asafoetida.
Cook until lentils turn golden.
Turn off the gas and let it cool down.
Now pour this mixture into the mixer grinder.
Add salt, tamarind, coconut and Brahmi leaves, add some water and grind it.
Grind it out in a bowl and serve.
Serve brahmi chutney with ghee dosa or dosa of your choice for breakfast.

Brahmi Chutney Recipe (Brahmi Chutney Recipe in Hindi)

Ingredients:

- 1 cup fresh Brahmi leaves (Bacopa monnieri)
- 1/2 cup grated coconut
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard seeds
- 1/2 teaspoon urad dal (split black gram)
- 1/2 teaspoon chana dal (split chickpea)
- 1/2 teaspoon asafoetida (hing)
- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder (haldi)
- 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder (lal mirch)
- Salt to taste
- 1 tablespoon oil

Instructions:

1. Wash the Brahmi leaves thoroughly and drain them well.

2. In a pan, heat the oil and add the mustard seeds, urad dal, and chana dal.

3. When the mustard seeds start to splutter, add the asafoetida and turmeric powder.

4. Add the grated coconut and sauté for a few seconds.

5. Add the washed Brahmi leaves, salt, and red chili powder.

6. Add water and let it boil for 5-7 minutes on medium heat.

7. Once the chutney has thickened, turn off the heat and let it cool down.

8. Serve the Brahmi Chutney with idli, dosa, or any Indian snack of your choice.

Enjoy the health benefits of Brahmi leaves with this delicious chutney!

reference_text:
**Gajar Vadi Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 cup wheat flour - 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 tablespoons curd, 2 teaspoons oil, 1 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 green chili - finely chopped, 1 teaspoon mustard, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 small Tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon sugar, salt - as per taste, 2 teaspoons sesame seeds (white),2 cups carrots - grated, 1-1 / 2 cups gram flour, 1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida, 1 tablespoon lemon juice

**Instructions:**
To make carrot vadi, first wash and peel the carrot.
After peeling, tighten the carrots and take them out in a bowl.
In the same bowl add gram flour, wheat flour, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder and add some water and take it.
Soft dough.
To make the vadi, sprinkle some water over the flour.
Take a little mixture in your hand and make a roll like a round cylinder.
Put the water in a saucepan to boil as per use.
Add the vadis and cook on high heat for 15 to 20 minutes.
Once cooked, take out the vadi and keep it aside to cool.
After cooling, cut it into small pieces.
Now for tempering, heat 2 teaspoons of oil in a pan.
Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and let it splutter.
After that add asafoetida, curry leaves, green chillies, sesame seeds and cook for 30 seconds.
Add vadis to it and mix well.
Serve.
Serve the Carrot Vadi Recipe with Masala Tea or Ginger Tea for the evening breakfast.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian dessert recipe for Thandai Kulfi Recipe - Holi Recipe
reference_text:
**Thandai Kulfi Recipe - Holi Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoons Poppy seeds,2 teaspoons Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),8 Whole Black Peppercorns,1/4 cup Badam (Almond) - blanched and peeled,1/4 cup Cashew nuts,1/4 cup Pistachios,3 liter Milk - (I use 2% fat),1/2 teaspoon Cinnamon Powder (Dalchini),1/2 cup Sugar,1 tablespoon Fennel seeds (Saunf)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Thandai Kulfi Recipe, first we will make a paste of the Thadai Masala by blending all the ingredients for the Thandai masala in a mixer grinder.
Keep this thandai masala aside.To begin making the Kulfi Recipe, the first step is to have to condense the milk.
For this heat milk in a heavy bottomed saucepan and bring it to boil.Once the milk comes to a boil, turn the heat to low and simmer the milk with almost continuous stirring and reduce the milk to more than half its quantity.
It is important to note that during the initial stages of milk condensation frequent stirring is important, but as it begins to start condensing and reaches less than half the quantity it is absolutely important that you stir continuously.
This is because condensed milk tends to get stuck to the bottom and sides of the pan and burn; leading to a very unpleasant taste.
Burning of milk is also toxic so you want to be extra careful.Once it the milk is reduced to more than half of its quantity, stir in the sugar until it dissolves completely.
Finally stir in the Thandai Paste Mix and give the mixture a good stir.
You can even use a hand blender to give it a good whisk.Turn off the heat and allow the kulfi mixture to cool completely.
Pour the thandai kulfi milk in the moulds until 3/4th full, seal them airtight and freeze them in the refrigerator.
The Thandai Kulfi takes a good fifteen hours or more to freeze.Once the Thandai Kulfi is frozen you can run the kulfi moulds under water to enable easy release on to the plate.Serve Thandai Kulfi Recipe as a Indian dessert for parties or even make it as a special dessert for the festival of holi.

This is because condensed milk tends to get stuck to the bottom and sides of the pan and burn; leading to a very unpleasant taste.
Burning of milk is also toxic so you want to be extra careful.Once it the milk is reduced to more than half of its quantity, stir in the sugar until it dissolves completely.
Finally stir in the Thandai Paste Mix and give the mixture a good stir.
You can even use a hand blender to give it a good whisk.Turn off the heat and allow the kulfi mixture to cool completely.
Pour the thandai kulfi milk in the moulds until 3/4th full, seal them airtight and freeze them in the refrigerator.
The Thandai Kulfi takes a good fifteen hours or more to freeze.Once the Thandai Kulfi is frozen you can run the kulfi moulds under water to enable easy release on to the plate.Serve Thandai Kulfi Recipe as a Indian dessert for parties or even make it as a special dessert for the festival of holi.

reference_text:
**Lauki Burfi With Paneer Recipe - Bottle Gourd Fudge With Cottage Cheese**


**Ingredients:**
3/4 cup Sugar,1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),250 gram Chenna (Homemade Cottage Cheese),1 tablespoon Ghee - for cooking,1/2 tablespoon Slivered Almonds (Badam),1 teaspoon Ghee - or butter for greasing,1 kg Bottle gourd (lauki)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Lauki Burfi With Paneer Recipe, place a pan on medium heat.
Add ghee to it.
Once the ghee is warm, add grated lauki to it.
Saute the lauki for 2-3 minutes till it becomes soft and starts releasing water.At this stage add in the sugar.
Mix well.
Saute the lauki and sugar mixture on medium heat till all the water from the lauki and sugar mixture is evaporated.At this stage, add the homemade chenna(paneer).
Add it to the lauki mixture and mix everything properly.Saute all the ingredients on low flame till all the ingredients are well combined for about 3-4 minutes.
Add cardamom powder and mix well.Turn off the heat and keep the pan aside.Meanwhile, grease a tray with some ghee and keep aside.Transfer the lauki mixture to the greased tray.
Spread it evenly.
Garnish it with some slivered almonds.
Let it cool for 10-15 minutes.
With a sharp knife, cut the cooled mixture into squares.Serve the healthy recipe of Lauki Burfi With Paneer Recipe as a nutritious sweet.
You can pack it up for your kids snack box or serve it after your everyday meals.
generated_text:
**Lauki Burfi with Paneer Recipe**


Ingredients:

- 1 cup grated bottle gourd (Lauki)

- 1 cup grated paneer (Cottage Cheese)

- 1/2 cup sugar

- 1/2 cup water

- 1/4 teaspoon cardamom powder

- 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg powder

- A pinch of saffron (optional)

- 2 tablespoons ghee

- 1 tablespoon cashew nuts, chopped

- 1 tablespoon raisins


1. In a pan, heat the ghee over medium heat. Add the chopped cashew nuts and raisins. Fry until the cashews turn golden brown and the raisins plump up. Remove from heat and set aside.


2. In the same pan, add the grated bottle gourd and fry for a few minutes until the excess moisture evaporates.


3. Add the sugar and water to the pan with the grated bottle gourd. Cook on medium heat until the sugar dissolves completely.


4. Add the cardamom powder, nutmeg powder, and saffron (if using) to the sugar syrup. Stir well to combine.


5. Add the grated paneer to the sugar syrup and mix gently. Cook for a few minutes until the paneer is well coated with the syrup.


6. Grease a baking tray with a little ghee. Pour the mixture onto the tray and spread it evenly.


7. Allow the mixture to cool for a few minutes. Then, using a knife, cut the burfi into small diamond shapes.


8. Garnish with the fried cashews and raisins before serving.


Enjoy your Lauki Burfi with Paneer as a sweet treat or as a snack with tea or coffee.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North East India Recipes appetizer recipe for Vegetarian Momo Recipe-Steamed Dumplings/A Street Food from the North East India
reference_text:
**Vegetarian Momo Recipe-Steamed Dumplings/A Street Food from the North East India**


**Ingredients:**
2 cups All Purpose Flour (Maida),Salt - to taste,1 inch Ginger - grated,Water - for kneading the dough,1 Onion,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1/2 cup Cabbage (Patta Gobi/ Muttaikose),1 Carrots (Gajjar)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Vegetarian Momo we will first knead the dough for the Vegetarian Momos cover.Method to make the momo coverAdd the flour into a large mixing bowl.
Add a little water at a time and knead to make a smooth dough.Once the dough comes together, keep kneading for about 5 minutes to make it smooth and elastic.
The dough should not be sticky.Cover the dough and keep it aside for half an hour.Method to make the filling for the momo You can use a turbo chop to chop all your vegetables individually or you can even finely chop them up.Once done combine all the chopped vegetables in a bowl.
Check, the salt levels and add the oil to it as well.
Mix well to combine.Our next step is to fill and shape the momosDivide the dough into small lemon size portions.
Keep the dough covered with a muslin cloth.Dust the dough in flour and roll out into a circle of 2 to 3 inch diameter.
Do not roll too thick, as the filling will push itself out and tear when steamed.Place a small spoon of filling in the center of the rolled circle.
Wet the edges with little water to help seal the momos.Bring together the edges of the dough with a pinching and folding motion on one side and bring the other side to hold to the pinched side.
By doing this, the half moon shaped momos will have a frilled look.
Proceed the similar way with the remaining dough.You can experiment with different shapes and sizes that you like.
All it takes is some time and practice.As you keep shaping the momos, you need to keep them on a greased platter and cover with a damp cloth to prevent them from drying out.While you are shaping the momos, keep a steamer with water boiling.
Grease the steamer pan with some oil to prevent the momos from sticking.Place the shaped vegetarian momos on the greased steamer pan.
So when you have one set of shaped momos, place them in the steamer and steam on high for 5 to 7 minutes until you see a glaze on the skin.
The skin will begin to shine.
As soon as you notice this, turn off the heat.Transfer the Vegetarian Momos from the steamer into a serving platter and serve hot.Serve the Vegetarian Momo Recipe along with Chilli Garlic Sauce as a Part Appetizer for any occasion with a party meal of Vegetarian Hakka Noodles,  Soy Chunks Manchurian Recipe In Sweet & Spicy Chinese Sauce and Toffee Banana.
generated_text:
**Vegetarian Momo Recipe - Steamed Dumplings**


**Ingredients:**

- 2 cups of refined flour (maida)

- 1/2 cup of wheat flour

- 1/2 teaspoon of salt

- 1 teaspoon of baking powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of baking soda

- 1/2 cup of water

- 1 tablespoon of oil


**For the Filling:**

- 1 cup of finely chopped cabbage

- 1/2 cup of finely chopped carrots

- 1/4 cup of finely chopped onions

- 1/4 cup of finely chopped green peas

- 1/2 teaspoon of salt

- 1/2 teaspoon of sugar

- 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder (optional)


**Instructions:**


1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the refined flour, wheat flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.

2. Add water gradually and mix until a soft dough forms. Add oil and knead for a few minutes until smooth. Cover the dough and let it rest for 15 minutes.

3. In a separate bowl, mix the chopped cabbage, carrots, onions, green peas, salt, sugar, and red chili powder (if using).

4. Roll out the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.

5. Place a small spoonful of the filling in the center of each dough ball.

6. Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges to seal.

7. Steam the momos over high heat for about 10-12 minutes or until they are cooked through and the dough is firm.

8. Serve the momos hot with a side of tomato sauce or a chili dip.


Enjoy your authentic North East Indian Vegetarian Momo app

**Kheer Puli Pitha | Bengali Pitha Recipe**

1 cup Fresh coconut - grated,1-1/2 teaspoons Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 pinch Salt,1 cup Hot water,200 grams Palm jaggery - crushed,1 tablespoon Cashew nuts - chopped (optional),2 tablespoons Ghee,100 grams Rice flour,1 liter Milk

**Instructions:**
For making Kheer Puli Pitha | Bengali Pitha Recipe, gather all the ingredients handy.Instruction for Making PuliSift rice flour, add a pinch of salt, ghee and hot water and knead to make a soft and supple dough.
Keep it covered with a clean kitchen towel.Heat a non-stick pan, add in crushed palm jaggery and allow jaggery to melt on medium heat.
Keep stirring to avoid charring.Once melted, add grated coconut, chopped cashews and half a teaspoon of cardamom powder.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian indian breakfast recipe for Palak (Spinach) Puri Recipe
reference_text:
**Palak (Spinach) Puri Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Water,1 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),1 Green Chilli - chopped,20 Spinach Leaves (Palak) - chopped,2 tablespoon Sunflower Oil - more to deep fry,1 cup Whole Wheat Flour,1/4 inch Ginger - peeled and chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin with the Palak Puri Recipe, first chop spinach, ginger and green chilies and boil them together.After boiling strain the water and leave it aside for 15 minutes till the water is drained.After cooling, take the boiled contents from the strainer and add to a mixer and make a thin puree.
Do not add water, just make a puree.Now in a mixing bowl add Vivatta whole wheat flour and the prepared pure, add some oil to the flour, cumin powder and salt.
Mix them to together to make dough.Do not add excess water as palak will release water on its own.
Now cover the dough with a muslin cloth and keep it aside for 30 minutes.After 30 minutes again, mould the dough and make small marble sized balls.Make small balls out of the dough and dust the rolling table with whole wheat flour and flatten the puris with the rolling pin.
See to that you make small thick size round out of the dough.As you have rolled all the dough now, heat a Kadai and add oil.
When oil gets heated, gently add a flattened puri.Using a frying spoon or slotted spoon gently press the puri, put some hot oil on top and move in a circular way.You will notice that the puri gets puffed and rises up.
At this point, turn it to the other side and fry till it turns golden brown.
Do not keep the puri in oil for more than 2 Minutes else it will become brittle.Drain it on kitchen towel.Continue the deep frying process for all the other flattened puris.Serve Palak Puris with Lahori Aloo and any raita of your choice.
generated_text:
Palak Puri Recipe


Ingredients:

- 2 cups of fresh spinach, finely chopped

- 1 cup of whole wheat flour (atta)

- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder (haldi)

- 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder (lal mirch)

- 1/2 teaspoon of coriander powder (dhania powder)

- 1/2 teaspoon of cumin powder (jeera powder)

- 1/4 teaspoon of asafoetida (hing)

- 1 teaspoon of salt (sendha namak)

- 1 cup of water

- 2 tablespoons of oil (for cooking)


1. Heat the oil in a pan and add the asafoetida. Stir for a few seconds.

2. Add the finely chopped spinach to the pan and cook on medium heat until the spinach wilts.

3. Once the spinach is cooked, add the turmeric, red chili, coriander, and cumin powders. Mix well.

4. Add salt and water to the mixture. Cook on low heat until the water is completely absorbed.

5. Let the mixture cool down. Once coo0led, add the spinach mixture to the whole wheat flour.

6. Knead the mixture with water to form a soft dough.

7. Divide the dough into small balls and roll them out into small circles.

8. Heat a tawa (griddle) and place a rolled puri on it.

9. Cook on both sides until they puff up and turn golden brown.

10. Serve hot with a side of chutney or pickle.


Enjoy your healthy and delicious Palak Puri for breakfast!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian lunch recipe for Pudina Quinoa Recipe
reference_text:
**Pudina Quinoa Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 onion - finely chopped, 6 cloves garlic - finely chopped, 1/4 mint leaves - finely chopped, 1 green chili - chopped,1 cup Kinua, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cardamom, 1 tsp Ghee, 2 long, salt

**Instructions:**
To make Pudina Kinua recipe, first wash 1 cup of Kinua thoroughly and soak it for 2 hours.
Heat the ghee in a pressure cooker.
After the ghee is hot, add garlic, onion and cook the onion to become soft.
Now add green chili, cinnamon, cinnamon, long, cardamom, salt and cook all on high flame for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water, close the cooker and cook till 2 cities come.
After coming to the city 2, reduce the heat and cook for 15 minutes.
Turn off the gas.
Let the pressure drain automatically.
After the pressure comes out, open the cooker and mix well.
Turn off the gas and put in a serving bowl.
Serve Pudina Kinua Recipe with Dhingri Peas and Tomato Cucumber Onion Raita for dinner.
generated_text:
Pudina Quinoa Recipe (Spinach Quinoa Salad)


Ingredients:

- 1 cup quinoa

- 2 cups water

- 1/2 teaspoon salt

- 1 tablespoon olive oil

- 2 cups fresh spinach, finely chopped

- 1/2 cup red onion, finely chopped

- 1/2 cup cherry tomatoes, halved

- 1/4 cup cucumber, diced

- 1/4 cup coriander leaves, finely chopped

- 1 tablespoon lemon juice

- Salt and pepper to taste


1. Rinse the quinoa under cold water until the water runs clear.

2. In a medium saucepan, combine the rinsed quinoa, water, and salt. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.

3. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15 minutes, or until all the water is absorbed and the quinoa is fluffy.

4. Remove from heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes.

5. Fluff the quinoa with a fork and let it cool to room temperature.

6. In a large bowl, combine the cooled quinoa with the chopped spinach, red onion, cherry tomatoes, and cucumber.

7. Drizzle the olive oil over the salad and toss gently to combine.

8. Add the chopped coriander leaves and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

9. Toss the salad again to evenly distribute all the ingredients.

10. Serve the Pudina Quinoa Salad as a refreshing and nutritious vegetarian lunch.


Enjoy your healthy and delicious vegetarian lunch!

**Andhra Style Bendakaya Charu Recipe - Ladies Finger Rasam**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Sugar,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,Salt - to taste,4 Garlic,1 teaspoon Ghee,6 Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra),1 cup Tamarind Water,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 Dry Red Chilli - broken into half,6 Pearl onions (Sambar Onions) - quartered,1/4 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 sprig Curry leaves,2 Tomato - roughly chopped

**Instructions:**
We begin making the Andhra Style Vendakaya Charu Recipe (Ladies Finger Rasam Recipe) by placing  a pressure cooker on medium flame with ghee and the mustard seeds and allow them to splutter, next add  cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, dry red chillies, curry leaves and allow them to crackle.
Next add in the chopped onions, and garlic pods.
Saute till it is translucent.
Then add in the tamarind water,  sugar, salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, asafoetida, coriander powderand chopped tomatoes and okra.
Stir well.Pressure cook the rasam for about 3 whistles, turn off the flame and allow the pressure to release naturally.
Serve the Andhra Style Vendakaya Charu Recipe (Ladies Finger Rasam Recipe) with  Chettinad Beans & Cauliflower Poriyal Recipe, hot steamed rice with a teaspoon of ghee to enrich the taste.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian dessert recipe for Rava Ladoo Recipe | Sooji Laddu Recipe
reference_text:
**Rava Ladoo Recipe | Sooji Laddu Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 teaspoons Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1/4 cup Cashew nuts - roughly halved,1/4 cup Raisins,1/2 cup Ghee - plus more if required,1 tablespoon Ghee,3/4 cup Sugar - finely powdered,2 cups Sooji (Semolina/ Rava) - semolina,1/4 cup Dessicated Coconut - (dry fine variery)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Rava Ladoo Recipe, we will first heat a teaspoon of ghee in a small pan on low heat; roast the cashew nuts until lightly golden and crisp.
Once roasted add in the raisins and sauté for a few seconds.
Turn off the heat and keep this aside.Preheat a pan over medium heat and roast the rava/sooji/ semolina for about 4 to 5 minutes until you get a roasted aroma.
Ensure it does not brown and burn.
Once the sooji is roasted, turn off the heat and keep aside.Into a mixer grinder, add the sugar and blend to make a fine powder.Add the roasted sooji/rava, dessicated coconut, elaichi powder to the powdered sugar and blend to make a coarse powder of the entire sooji mixture.Powdering the rava/sooji will a little will give a better texture and good bite for the rava ladoo.
Into a large mixing bowl, add the sugar, rava, coconut mixture, roasted cashew nuts and raisins.
Give the mixture a stir.Add the melted ghee and combine it well together.
Shape the rava ladoo mixture into 20 equal size balls.
The entire mixture should be warm, if the rava mixture has cooled down, then it will not be able to hold firm into a ball.
If you are unable to hold the mixture and shape it into a ball; add in a tablespoon of milk or ghee or both to help it come together.You have to ensure the you compress the rava ladoo mixture effectively with your fingers to bring it together.
 As you keep compressing the rava ladoo, it will get more and more firm and you will be able to shape it into a ball.
Once you have shaped all the Rava Ladoos, they are ready to be served.
Store them in an airtight container for about 2 weeks.Serve the Rava Ladoo as a dessert after your everyday meal of Punjabi Dal Tadka, Arbi Shimla Mirch Sabzi and Phulka with your family.
generated_text:
**Vegetarian Indian Dessert: Rava Ladoo Recipe**


**Ingredients:**

- 1 cup sooji (rava or semolina)

- 1/2 cup ghee (clarified butter)

- 1/2 cup sugar

- 1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder

- A pinch of saffron (optional)

- A few strands of saffron (optional)

- Water

- Ghee or oil for frying


1. Heat the ghee in a pan over medium heat.

2. Add the sooji to the pan and roast it for about 2-3 minutes, constantly stirring to avoid burning.

3. Once the sooji turns golden brown, remove it from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.

4. In a separate pan, heat water and add the saffron strands. Let it steep for a minute to infuse the color and flavor.

5. Add the sugar to the saffron water and heat it on low flame until the sugar dissolves completely.

6. Add the cardamom powder to the sugar syrup and mix well.

7. Now, add the roasted sooji to the sugar syrup and mix thoroughly.

8. Once the mixture cools down a bit, start forming small balls with your hands.

9. Roll the ladoos in ghee or oil to give them a nice shine and to prevent them from drying out.

10. Serve the Rava Ladoo at room temperature or chilled.


Enjoy your homemade Vegetarian Indian dessert, Rava Ladoo!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian South Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Bhindi Huli Recipe - South Indian Style Tangy Okra
reference_text:
**Bhindi Huli Recipe - South Indian Style Tangy Okra**


**Ingredients:**
2 cups Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) - chopped,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,2 tablespoon Coconut Oil,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1/2 teaspoon Tamarind Paste,1 teaspoon Sambar Powder,1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 cup Onion - chopped or cubed

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Bhindi Huli Recipe (South Indian Style Tangy Okra), wash and dry okra; trim the ends and cut into 1 inch pieces and keep aside.
Cube onions and keep aside.Heat oil in a large deep saucepan and once hot, add mustard seeds.
As soon as the mustard seeds crackle and pop, stir in the chopped onions and mix well.Add in the turmeric powder, red chilli powder and sambar powder (you can omit this or use any curry powder you have).
Mix well and sauté on medium high heat, stirring often until the onions are soft and lightly sautéed or until they change color.Add the chopped bhindi/okra and mix well.
Stir in tamarind water or paste along with salt to taste and mix again.Add as much or as little tamarind for flavoring depending upon how sour you want your curry.
Cook on medium high, covered, stirring in between until the bhindi is cooked and semi dry.Taste in between and add salt or more tamarind according to your taste.
Remove from heat and serve as a side dish with your meal.You can serve Bhindi Huli Recipe (South Indian Style Tangy Okra) along with Mixed Vegetable Sambar and Steamed Rice for a weekday meal.

Bhindi Huli Recipe - South Indian Style Tangy Okra

Ingredients:

- 500 grams of okra (bhindi)
- 1 tablespoon of oil
- 1 teaspoon of mustard seeds
- 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds
- 1 teaspoon of asafoetida (hing)
- 1 small red onion, finely chopped
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder
- 1 teaspoon of red chili powder
- 1 teaspoon of coriander powder
- 1 teaspoon of garam masala
- Salt to taste
- 1/2 cup of water
- Fresh coriander leaves (cilantro) for garnish

Instructions:

1. Wash the okra thoroughly and pat dry with a kitchen towel. Remove the ends of the okra and cut them into 1-inch pieces.

2. Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and asafoetida. Stir for a few seconds until the seeds start to splutter.

3. Add the finely chopped onion and green chilies to the pan. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.

4. Add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala to the pan. Mix well to combine.

5. Add the okra pieces to the pan and mix well to coat them with the spice mixture.

6. Add salt to taste and mix well.

7. Add 1/2 cup of water to the pan and cover it with a lid. Reduce the heat to low and cook for 10-15 minutes until the okra is tender and the water has evaporated.

8. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves (cilant0r) and serve hot with rice or roti.

Enjoy your delicious and tangy South Indian style bhindi huli recipe!
ref_name: Bhindi Huli Recipe - South Indian Style Tangy Okra
ref_instructions:
To begin making the Bhindi Huli Recipe (South Indian Style Tangy Okra), wash and dry okra; trim the ends and cut into 1 inch pieces and keep aside.
Cube onions and keep aside.Heat oil in a large deep saucepan and once hot, add mustard seeds.
As soon as the mustard seeds crackle and pop, stir in the chopped onions and mix well.Add in the turmeric powder, red chilli powder and sambar powder (you can omit this or use any curry powder you have).
Mix well and sauté on medium high heat, stirring often until the onions are soft and lightly sautéed or until they change color.Add the chopped bhindi/okra and mix well.
Stir in tamarind water or paste along with salt to taste and mix again.Add as much or as little tamarind for flavoring depending upon how sour you want your curry.
Cook on medium high, covered, stirring in between until the bhindi is cooked and semi dry.Taste in between and add salt or more tamarind according to your taste.
Remove from heat and serve as a side dish with your meal.You can serve Bhindi Huli Recipe (South Indian Style Tangy Okra) along with Mixed Vegetable Sambar and Steamed Rice for a weekday meal.

reference_text:
**Pineapple Mushroom Tikka Recipe With Tandoori Masala**


**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - diced,250 grams Button mushrooms,Salt - as per your taste,1 teaspoon Ginger Garlic Paste,1 tablespoon Tandoori masala,1 teaspoon Lemon juice,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/4 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),2 Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) - diced,1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 cup Pineapple - diced,2 tablespoons Sunflower Oil,3 tablespoons Gram flour (besan),1 teaspoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Pineapple Mushroom Tikka, wash the mushrooms well, deseed the capsicum and cut into squares.
Chop onions and pineapple into thin squares - keep aside.
Take all the chopped capsicum, onion, pineapple and mushroom pieces in a wide mixing bowl.Add besan flour, curd, salt, ginger garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, tandoori masala powder, Kasuri methi, lemon juice and a tsp of oil to it.Mix and marinate so the tandoori masala paste coats well.
Cover and refrigerate for an hour.
After an hour, mix well once and insert marinated mushrooms, capsicum, pineapple and onions alternatively into wooden skewers.Heat a griddle and sprinkle some oil.
Place the skewers and roast Pineapple Mushroom Tikka well in medium flame turning on all sides.
Add little oil on top while turning.Once the Pineapple Mushroom Tikka turns golden on all sides, remove from griddle and arrange in serving plate.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Goan Recipes side dish recipe for Goan Raw Banana Sabzi Recipe
reference_text:
**Goan Raw Banana Sabzi Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
3 Raw Banana - chopped to cubes,1 Dry Red Chilli,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 teaspoons Sunflower Oil,4 tablespoons Fresh coconut - grated,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1 Onion - finely chopped,1 sprig Curry leaves

**Instructions:**
To begin making Goan Raw Banana Sabzi Recipe, peel the skin of the plantains, chop them into cubes and keep it immersed in cold water in a bowl until further cooking.Heat oil in a pan, once the oil is hot, add the mustard seeds and let it splutter.Add the red chilli, curry leaves and let it crackle.
Add the onions and saute until it turns translucent.Drain the plantains and add it to the pan.
Saute.Add turmeric powder, salt, mix well and let the plantains cook with the lid closed for 5 minutes.
The plantains cook really fast.Now, pound the cumin and grated coconut using a pestle and mortar to a course mix and add it to the cooked plantains.Mix well and saute for another 5 minutes and switch off the heat.Serve the Goan Raw Banana Sabzi Recipe along with Phulka, Steamed Rice and Feijoada Recipe (Spicy Goan Black Eyed Peas Stew) for a rainy weeknight dinner.

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly Maharashtrian Recipes indian breakfast recipe for Maharashtrian Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe - Spiced Cucumber Flat Bread
reference_text:
**Maharashtrian Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe - Spiced Cucumber Flat Bread**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),5 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1/2 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,1 Cucumber - peeled and grated,Salt - to taste,1/2 cup Whole Wheat Flour,1/2 cup Gram flour (besan),1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1/4 cup Rice flour,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Ghee - for smearing on thalipeeth

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Maharashtrian Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe, in a large mixing bowl add all the 3 flours together and mix them.
Add all the remaining ingredients like the grated cucumber, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, green chillies, coriander leaves, garlic and salt to taste.Mix everything well and add a little water at a time to make a smooth and firm dough of the Kakdi Thalipeeth.
The cucumber will release water when it gets combined with salt, so take care to add little water at a time.
The dough will be slightly sticky to handle.
Allow the dough to rest for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 portions.
Preheat the skillet on medium heat.
Keep water in a separate bowl to wet fingers and to wet a muslin cloth.Spread a wet muslin cloth on a smooth counter top.
Take a portion of the Kakdi Thalipeeth mixture and pat it on the wet cloth to about 4 inches in diameter.
Try not to make it too thin as it can break while flipping it on the skillet.Carefully lift the cloth along with the patted mixture and flip the Thalipeeth over on the skillet.
Drizzle a teaspoon of oil or ghee around the Thalipeeth and cook on medium heat.After about 3 minutes, you will be able to flip the Kakdi Thalipeeth over to the other side.
Allow each side to cook on medium heat for about 3-4 minutes, until the Thalipeeth are slightly crisp and browned.
Remove from heat and place on a serving platter.Continue the same procedure with the other portions of the Kakdi Thalipeeth dough.Serve the Maharashtrian Kakdi Thalipeeth along with Homemade Yogurt or lahsun chutney for breakfast.
generated_text:
**Maharashtrian Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe - Spiced Cucumber Flat Bread**


**Ingredients:**

- 2 cups of finely chopped cucumber

- 1 cup of whole wheat flour

- 1/2 cup of besan (gram flour)

- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of coriander powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of cumin powder

- 1/4 teaspoon of asafoetida (hing)

- Salt to taste

- Water as needed

- Oil for cooking


1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the finely chopped cucumber with the whole wheat flour, besan, turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, asafoetida, and salt.

2. Mix all the ingredients thoroughly until a dough forms. If the mixture is too dry, add a little water to make it pliable.

3. Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let it rest for about 15 minutes.

4. After resting, knead the dough gently for a few minutes until it becomes smooth.

5. Divide the dough into small balls, flatten them slightly, and roll them into flat circles.

6. Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat and lightly grease it with oil.

7. Place a rolled-out dough on the pan and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side or until you see golden brown spots.

8. Serve the Kakdi Thalipeeth hot with a side of yogurt or a chutney of your choice.


This recipe is diabetic-friendly as it is low in sugar and high in fiber, which helps in controlling blood sugar levels.

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Dahi Batata Puri Chaat Recipe - Classic Indian Tea Time Snack
reference_text:
**Dahi Batata Puri Chaat Recipe - Classic Indian Tea Time Snack**


**Ingredients:**
Chaat Masala Powder - powder (garnish),1 tablespoon Sugar,1/2 cup Green Chutney (Coriander & Mint),1 teaspoon Lemon juice,2 Onions - finely chopped,1/2 cup Sweet Chutney (Date & Tamarind),1/2 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt) - whisked (optional),1/4 cup Mango (Raw) - finely chopped(optional if not in season),Black Salt (Kala Namak) - o taste,1 cup Sev,Red Chilli powder - optional (garnish),24 Pani Puris,2 Potatoes (Aloo) - boiled and cubed or sliced,1 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Dahi Batata Puri Chaat Recipe, first prep all the ingredients and keep them ready.
 In a pressure cooker, add the potatoes and required water.
Close the cooker and pressure cook it for 2 to 3 whistles.
Let the pressure release immediately.
Take out the potatoes and let them cool down.
Once it is cooled down, peel the potatoes and cut them in squares.
Keep it aside.Make the Garlic Chutney, Green Chutney and Date and Tamarind Chutney and keep them aside.Once you have all the ingredients prepped.
In a mixing bowl, combine the boiled potatoes, raw mango, chaat masala powder  and juice from one lemon.
Stir well to combine and keep them aside.Whisk the yogurt/ dahi ingredients and keep aside.Arrange the puris or papadi on a serving plate.
Gently make a hole in the puri with a spoon or your fingers.
Spoon in small portions of the potato mixture into each one of the puris.Add the onions, the dahi into each of the puri.
Finally, add the chutneys and sprinkle with sev, any additional chaat masala powder and red chili powder and coriander leaves and serve.Serve Dahi Batata Puri Chaat Recipe along with Bhel Puri and Bombay Masala Sandwich and a cup of Masala Chai for your Chaat Party.

user_message: Generate an Indo Chinese recipe for Szechwan Hakka Noodle Momo Recipe
reference_text:
**Szechwan Hakka Noodle Momo Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 Carrot (Gajjar) - finely chopped,1 cup Hakka Noodles - boiled,1 cup All Purpose Flour (Maida),Salt - to taste,Water - to knead,1 Onion - finely chopped,3 tablespoons Schezwan sauce,5 Stalks Spring Onion (Bulb & Greens) - finely chopped,1 Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Black pepper powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Szechwan Hakka Noodle Momo Recipe, first let’s make the dough first.For the Momo doughIn a mixing bowl, take maida add water slowly and knead to make a firm and smooth dough.
The dough should not be sticky.
Keep the momo dough covered for 2 hours before making corn stuffing.For the FillingHeat a sauce pan with oil, add chopped ginger and garlic.
Saute till for a few seconds and add chopped onions.Saute until the onions are translucent.
Add in chopped carrot and bell peppers and keep stir frying until the vegetables are cooked.Add salt, pepper and Szechwan paste and mix well.
Once the spices are mixed you can add cooked noodles and give it a stir fry.
Check for seasoning and if you require more you are free to add.
Switch off the heat and allow the Szechwan momo filling to cool down.
To make the MomoTake a bamboo dim sum maker, or the regular idli steamer and fill it with water and place it over a medium heat.Later divide the dough into equal portions, to the size of a lemon.Take each ball and start rolling it out on a surface.
Roll out the dough as thin so that the outer layer of the momo is thin and delicate.In every flatten dough leaf, add 1 tablespoon of the filling and fold the edges to semi circles and start pleating to give a nice characteristic pleats.Do the same with all the rest of the dough and momo filling.
Keep the completed Szechwan noodle momos under a moist cloth to prevent drying.Grease the steamer with oil or place butter paper before placing Szechwan noodle momo from prevent sticking.If using traditional steamer it has own boiler or if using bamboo steamer then boil water in a shallow pan and when water starts boiling place the momo filled bamboo steamer on it.
Steam for 10 - 15 minutes.Serve the Szechwan Hakka Noodle Momo along with Basil And Mushroom Toast With Thai Red Chilli Recipe and Thai Green Papaya Salad Recipe to complete your meal for your.
Sunday lunch.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Andhra south indian breakfast recipe for Andhra Style Allam Pachadi (Ginger Chutney Recipe)
reference_text:
**Andhra Style Allam Pachadi (Ginger Chutney Recipe)**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds,Sunflower Oil - for cooking,Salt - to taste,1 tablespoon White Urad Dal (Split),1 Onion - chopped,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,2 Dry Red Chillies,1 tablespoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1 Tomato - chopped,1 sprig Curry leaves,1 pinch Asafoetida (hing),3 inch Ginger - chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Andhra Style Allam Pachadi, heat a kadai with oil, add the dals and saute them till they are light brown.Add the dry red chillies, coriander seeds, onions, ginger and saute them till the onions are translucent.Once done, add the tomatoes and salt and saute till the tomato becomes mushy.
Switch off the heat and grind it to a smooth paste.To make the tempering, heat a small kadai with oil, add mustard seeds and allow it to splutter.Add the curry leaves and hing, leave it for 10 seconds and pour it over the chutney.
Serve the Andhra Style Allam Pachadi along with pesarattu or idli to enjoy your morning breakfast.
generated_text:
Andhra Style Allam Pachadi (Ginger Chutney Recipe)


Ingredients:

- 2 cups of fresh ginger, peeled and finely chopped

- 1/2 cup of fresh green chilies, finely chopped

- 1/2 cup of tamarind pulp

- 1/4 cup of jaggery (optional, for a less spicy chutney)

- 1/2 teaspoon of mustard seeds

- 1/2 teaspoon of urad dal (split black gram)

- 1/2 teaspoon of chana dal (split chickpea)

- A pinch of asafoetida (hing)

- A few curry leaves

- Salt to taste

- Water as needed


1. In a pan, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds, urad dal, and chana dal. Stir-fry until the dals turn golden brown.

2. Add the asafoetida and curry leaves. Stir for a few seconds until the curry leaves become fragrant.

3. Add the finely chopped ginger and green chilies to the pan. Sauté for a minute until the ginger and chilies are slightly softened.

4. Add the tamarind pulp and jaggery (if using). Mix well and cook for another 2-3 minutes.

5. Add salt to taste and a little water if needed to adjust the consistency.

6. Let the chutney cool down a bit before serving.

7. Serve the Andhra Style Allam Pachadi with hot rice or Indian flatbreads like roti or paratha.


Enjoy this tangy and spicy ginger chutney as a refreshing start to your day or as a side dish with your breakfast.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian South Indian Recipes dessert recipe for Ada Pradhaman Recipe With Homemade Ada- Kerala Special Payasam Recipe
reference_text:
**Ada Pradhaman Recipe With Homemade Ada- Kerala Special Payasam Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
3/4 cup Jaggery - powdered,10 Raisins,1/4 cup Hot water,1/4 cup Fresh coconut - finely chopped or thinly sliced coconut pieces,1 Banana leaf,1 teaspoon Ghee,1/2 teaspoon Ghee,Salt - a pinch,1/2 teaspoon Sunflower Oil - for greasing,2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds - powdered,10 Cashew nuts,1/4 cup Rice flour,1 cup Coconut milk - thick one

**Instructions:**
To prepare Ada Pradhaman Recipe With Homemade Ada, let us first make the ada.To make homemade rice ada, in a mixing bowl, combine rice flour with a pinch of salt in a bowl and pour boiling hot water into the flour and combine it to make a dough using a spoon/spatula.
Preheat a steamer, to steam the adas.
Grease a banana leaf with oil and spread the rice dough into a thin layer on half of the banana leaf.Close the ada using the other side of the banana leaf.Steam cook this for 10-15 minutes.Once cool, remove the ada from the leaf and cut into smaller pieces to look flaky.To proceed with payasam recipe, heat a pan with ghee on low flame add the cashews, coconut, and raisins till they are golden.
Turn off the flame and set aside.
Heat a saucepan, on medium heat, add jaggery with 3/4 cup of water, heat till the jaggery dissolves, once it is well dissolved, turn off the flame.
 Pass the jaggery syrup through a strainer and set aside.Heat another saucepan,  add chopped ada , and dry roast the ada for 2-3 minutes, now add the jaggery syrup to it.
Let the ada cook in jaggery water over low flame for 7-8 minutes or until the aggery syrup thickens.
At this stage add the cardamom powder, coconut milk and turn off the flame.
Make sure not to cook after adding coconut milk.Garnish with the fried cashews, coconut, and raisins.Your delicious Ada Pradhaman Recipe With Homemade Ada is ready to be served during the festive lunch!
generated_text:
Ingredients:

- 1 cup rice
- 1/2 cup cashews
- 1/2 cup jaggery
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder
- 1/2 teaspoon ghee
- 1/2 teaspoon rose water
- 1/2 teaspoon saffron strands
- 1/2 cup coconut milk
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder
- 1/2 teaspoon ghee
- 1/2 teaspoon rose water
- 1/2 teaspoon saffron strands

Instructions:

1. Soak the rice and cashews in water for 30 minutes.

2. Drain the water and grind the rice and cashews into a fine paste.

3. In a pan, add the jaggery and water. Cook on medium heat until the jaggery melts and turns into a thick syrup.

4. Add the rice and cashew paste to the jaggery syrup. Cook on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

5. Add the milk and cook on low heat for another 10 minutes.

6. Add the cardamom powder and ghee to the rice mixture. Stir well.

7. In a small bowl, soak the saffron strands in rose water.

8. Add the saffron-rose water mixture to the rice mixture. Stir well.

9. In a separate pan, add the coconut milk and water. Cook on low heat until the coconut milk thickens slightly.

10. Add the cardamom powder and ghee to the coconut milk mixture. Stir well.

11. Add the coconut milk mixture to the rice mixture. Stir well.

12. Cook on low heat for another 10 minutes.

13. Serve hot or chilled.

Enjoy your delicious Ada Pradhaman, a traditional South Indian dessert made with rice,

reference_text:
**Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari Recipe- With Pomegranates**


**Ingredients:**
Lemon juice - as required,1/2 cup Carrots (Gajjar) - grated,2 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,Salt - to taste,1/2 cup Fresh Pomegranate Fruit Kernels,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1 to 2 tablespoons Fresh coconut - grated (optional),1 sprig Curry leaves,2 to 4 Green Chillies - chopped,Sunflower Oil - as required,1 pinch Asafoetida (hing),1 cup Green Moong Sprouts

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari recipe, we need to keep the green moong sprouts ready.
We can either use store bought Sprouts or learn How to make Sprouts at Home.
In a big bowl, combine the  sprouted moong, grated carrot, pomegranate, green chillies, grated coconut, coriander leaves, salt and lime.In a tadka pan heat oil on medium flame and once it's hot add mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add in the asafoetida.
Turn off the flame.
Pour the tempering on the Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari.
Healthy, nutritious and delicious Sprouted moong salad is ready to serve.Serve Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari along with Tomato Onion Sambar and Steamed Rice and Raw Papaya Thoran Recipe for a weekday meal.
generated_text:
**Diabetic Friendly Karnataka Side Dish: Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari with Pomegranates**


**Ingredients:**

- 1 cup sprouted green gram (moong dal)

- 1 pomegranate, peeled and seeds removed

- 1/2 cup chopped cucumber

- 1/2 cup chopped tomato

- 1/4 cup chopped onion

- 1/4 cup chopped coriander leaves

- 1/4 cup chopped mint leaves

- 1/2 teaspoon mustard seeds

- 1/2 teaspoon urad dal

- A pinch of asafoetida (hing)

- Salt to taste

- 1/2 teaspoon sugar substitute (optional)

- Lemon juice from half a lemon

- Water as needed


1. Rinse the sprouted green gram thoroughly under cold water.

2. In a large bowl, combine the sprouted green gram with chopped cucumber, tomato, and onion.

3. Add the chopped coriander and mint leaves to the bowl.

4. In a small pan, heat a teaspoon of oil and add mustard seeds and urad dal.

5. When the mustard seeds start to splutter, add a pinch of asafoetida and stir for a few seconds.

6. Pour this tempering over the sprouts and vegetables.

7. Add salt to taste and the sugar substitute if desired.

8. Squeeze the lemon juice over the mixture.

9. Add water gradually and mix well to make a thick kosambari.

10. Garnish with pomegranate seeds and serve chilled.


This kosambari is a healthy, diabetic-friendly side dish that is low in carbohydrates and high in fiber, making it suitable for a balanced diet.

reference_text:
**Sweet Potato & Peas Cutlet Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 tablespoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder),1 tablespoon Lemon juice,Salt - to taste,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - few,3 Sweet Potato - boiled or roasted and mashed,1 tablespoon Chaat Masala Powder,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1/4 tablespoon Garam masala powder,1 cup Whole Wheat Bread crumbs,1 tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste,1/4 cup Water,2 tablespoon Corn flour,1 tablespoon Cumin powder (Jeera),Sunflower Oil - to shallow fry,1/2 cup Green peas (Matar) - boiled and mashed

**Instructions:**
To prepare Sweet Potato & Peas Cutlet Recipe, take a bowl and mix mashed peas and cooked sweet potato.Add ginger garlic paste, green chillies, and fresh coriander, mix well.Add all the dry ingredients, chaat masala, salt, chilli powder, roasted cumin powder, dry mango powder, garam masala.Add lime juice and mix everything well.Also, mix corn flour and water in a small bowl.Shape the mixture into medium size patties.Dip the patty in corn flour mixture and roll into the breadcrumbs, making sure that the bread crumbs cover the cutlet well.
Repeat for a batch of 3-4 patties at a time.Heat oil in a pan and shallow fry them on both sides on low heat until golden brown.Serve Sweet Potato & Peas Cutlet Recipe hot with dhaniya pudina chutney and dates and tamarind chutney or roasted tomato sauce.
generated_text:
**Sweet Potato & Peas Cutlet Recipe**


Ingredients:

- 2 medium-sized sweet potatoes, peeled and grated

- 1 cup of fresh or frozen green peas

- 1 small onion, finely chopped

- 2 green chilies, finely chopped (optional)

- 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds

- 1 teaspoon of coriander seeds

- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder

- Salt to taste

- 1 cup of all-purpose flour

- 1/2 cup of besan (gram flour)

- 1/2 cup of water

- 2 tablespoons of cooking oil


1. Heat a pan and add the cumin and coriander seeds. Roast them until they turn aromatic.

2. Once the seeds are fragrant, grind them to a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.

3. In a pan, heat the oil and add the finely chopped onion. Sauté until translucent.

4. Add the green chilies (if using) and grated sweet potatoes to the pan. Cook for a few minutes until the sweet potatoes are soft.

5. Add the peas, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for another 2-3 minutes.

6. In a bowl, mix the all-purpose flour, besan, and water to form a smooth batter.

7. Add the sweet potato and peas mixture to the batter and combine well.

8. Heat a non-stick pan and add a little oil.

9. Shape the batter into small round cutlets and flatten them slightly.

10. Cook the cutlets on medium heat until both sides are golden brown and crispy.

11. Serve the sweet potato and peas cutlets hot with a side of green chutney or ketchup.


Enjoy your healthy and delicious vegetarian snack!

reference_text:
**Chettinad Keerai Mandi Recipe With Amaranth Greens**


**Ingredients:**
2 cups Green Amaranth Leaves - cleaned and finely chopped,1 cup Mandi - (leftover water from washing rice),1 Green Chilli - finely chopped,Salt - to taste,1/2 teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split),1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,2 teaspoon Sesame (Gingelly) Oil,2 Dry Red Chillies,1/4 cup Coconut milk - (optional but recommended),8 Shallots - finely chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making Chettinad Keerai Mandi Recipe, clean, wash and finely chop the amaranth leaves or any greens of your choice and keep aside.Collect one cup of mandi water by washing the raw rice and keep aside.Heat oil in a pan on medium flame, add the mustard seeds, urad dal and let it crackle.Add the dried red chillies and let it splutter.Add the shallots, green chilly and saute until the onions turn translucent.Add the amaranth greens, season with salt and saute until the greens wilts.Add the mandi water and simmer for 2 minutes.Now, add the coconut milk, bring it to a rolling boil and switch off the flame.Serve the Chettinad Keerai Mandi Recipe along with Steamed Rice, Paruppu Urundai Kuzhambu and Beans Poriyal to make it a complete Chettinad style lunch over the weekend.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Hariyali Dum Aloo Recipe - Potatoes In Spinach Mint Curry
reference_text:
**Hariyali Dum Aloo Recipe - Potatoes In Spinach Mint Curry**


**Ingredients:**
2 Green Chillies,4 cloves Garlic,Salt - to taste,1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),1/8 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,200 grams Baby Potatoes - boiled and cubed (regular potatoes also work),1 inch Ginger,2 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 clove Onion - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Ghee,1 tablespoon Fresh cream,500 grams Spinach,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 cup Mint Leaves (Pudina)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Hariyali Dum Aloo Recipe, first boil potatoes with required salt in a sauce pan with a lid.Once cooked, peel the skin and cut into small 2 inch cubes if you are using regular potatoes.
If you are using baby potatoes, you can only cut the very large ones.The next step for the Hariyali dum aloo is to cook the spinach and mint.Into the pressure cooker, add the spinach, mint leaves, green chilli, garlic, ginger, salt and 3 tablespoons of water.
Pressure cook for 2 whistles and turn off the heat.
Release the pressure immediately, by running it under cold water to prevent the spinach leaves from discoloring.
 Once cooled, add the hariyali base made from spinach and mint into a mixer grinder.
Blend to make a smooth puree and keep aside.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan and add turmeric powder, red chilli powder and salt.
Add the boiled potatoes and fry them till golden crust forms on surface.
Keep it aside.Heat ghee in a heavy bottomed pan.
Add cinnamon stick, garlic, onions and saute until the onions soften.Once the onions soften, add the garam masala powder, the ground hariyali spinach and mint base and the roasted potatoes.
 Add a tablespoon of cream.
Simmer the Hariyali Dum Aloo Recipe for 3 to 4 minutes and turn off the heat.
Transfer the dum aloo to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Hariyali Dum Aloo with Jeera Rice, Peshawari Naan and Boondi Raita Recipe Spiced With Black Salt to make it a complete meal.

Hariyali Dum Aloo Recipe - Potatoes In Spinach Mint Curry

- 1 teaspoon of coriander powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of garam masala
- 1/2 cup of fresh spinach, finely chopped
- 1/4 cup of fresh mint leaves, finely chopped
- Salt to taste
- 1/2 cup of water
- Fresh coriander leaves, for garnish

Instructions:

1. Heat the ghee or oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the cumin and mustard seeds and let them splutter.

2. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until they turn golden brown.

3. Add the finely chopped tomatoes and cook until they are soft and mushy.

4. Add the turmeric powder, coriander powder, and red chili powder. Mix well and cook for a minute.

8. Add the garam masala and mix well.

9. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot with roti or naan.

5 Garlic - cloves,1 Green Chilli - finely chopped,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 cup Rajma (Large Kidney Beans) - boiled,Asafoetida (hing) - A pinch,1/2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,2 cups Spinach Leaves (Palak) - roughly chopped,1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Sunflower Oil - as required

To begin making Rajma Saagwala recipe, pressure cook the overnight soaked rajma for two whistles in a pressure cooker and keep it aside.Now heat a wok in medium flame, pour oil, add the cumin seeds and a pinch of asafoetida and let it crackle.Add the finely chopped garlic and sauté till aromatic.Add the finely chopped onion and green chilli and cook till the onions become translucent.Add the roughly chopped spinach and cooked rajma and give it a stir.Now add the turmeric, garam masala powder and season with salt and mix well.Let the vegetable cook in medium flame for 10 minutes, occasionally keep stirring in between.Once done, switch off the flame and it is ready to be served.
Serve Rajma Saagwala as a side dish with Rabodi Ki Sabji and Whole Wheat Lachha Parathas for a delicious weekday meal.

reference_text:
**Mutton Keema Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - as required,1/2 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),Mint Leaves (Pudina) - chopped,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),5 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,3 Onion - finely chopped,3 Tomatoes - finely chopped,2 cup Mutton - minced,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,2 Bay leaves (tej patta),2 tablespoons Ginger Garlic Paste,1-1/2 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania) - chopped,1/2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 tablespoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder,Sunflower Oil - as required,1 teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Mutton Keema recipe, Heat oil in a wide pan and add cumin seeds, bay leaves, peppercorns and cardamoms.Add the chopped onions and saute them till they turn brown in colour.Add garlic and ginger paste, green chillies, tomato puree and mix everything well.Let it cook for about 5 minutes on medium flame.
Once it is done, add coriander powder, red chilli powder, cumin powder and salt.Cook for about 5-8 minutes on low flame.
After 5-8 minutes, add the minced mutton and salute it for about 2-3 minutes.
Add required water and cook till it gets dry.Once the mutton is cooked, switch off the stove and add garam masala.
Mix well and garnish it with mint leaves.In Maharashtra, this Mutton Keema is served with pav.
You can also serve it with Phulkas or Pakki Mutton Biryani.

reference_text:
**Amla Rasam Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 tomato,6 amla, 1/4 cup green coriander - chopped, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1/4 teaspoon ginger, 2 teaspoons ghee, 2 green chillies, 1/2 teaspoon black pepper powder, asafoetida - a pinch, 1 teaspoon mustard, 1 dry red chili - break, salt - as per taste, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric powder, 10-15 mint leaves

**Instructions:**
To make the Nellikai Rasam recipe, first of all, pour the amla in a saucepan with 2 cups of water.
Cook on low heat.
After the amla becomes soft, drain the water, turn off the gas and keep it aside.
Now in a mixer grinder, add tomato, ginger, mint, coriander, green chili, cumin, black pepper powder and grind it.
Keep it separately.
Add the cooked amla, 3 cups of water, turmeric powder in a pan and allow it to boil on low heat.
After boiling, add ground spices, salt and mix.
Let it boil After that turn off the gas and keep it aside.
Now heat the ghee in a tempering pan for tempering.
Add mustard seeds and let it splutter.
After this add dry red chillies, asafoetida and cook for 15 seconds.
Add this tempering to the rasam and serve.
Serve Nellikai Rasam with Thoran of Carrot and Beans, Rice and Keerai Sambar.

4. In a pan, heat ghee and add mustard seeds, urad dal, chana dal, jeera, and asafoetida.

5. Once the mustard seeds start to splutter, add turmeric powder, red chili powder, and sugar.

6. Add the mashed amla pieces, tamarind paste, and salt. Mix well.

7. Add water and bring it to a boil.

8. Add coriander powder, coriander seeds, and fenugreek seeds. Mix well.

9. Let the rasam simmer for 5 minutes on low heat.

10. Add lemon juice and mix well.

11. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot with rice or Indian flatbreads.

Enjoy your delicious and healthy Amla Rasam!

user_message: Generate a Parsi Recipes recipe for Parsi Mutton Cutlets Recipe
reference_text:
**Parsi Mutton Cutlets Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
4 tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 Cloves (Laung) -,1 Potato (Aloo) - boiled and mashed,1/2 tablespoon Ginger - paste,1/2 tablespoon Garlic - paste,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1 Onion - finely chopped,Salt - accroding to taste,3 tablespoon Mint Leaves (Pudina) - finely chopped,1/2 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini) - powdered,2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,Whole Wheat Bread crumbs - as required,Sunflower Oil - required for frying,500 grams Mutton - minced

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Parsi Mutton Cutlets Recipe, remove the moisture from minced mutton by pressing it between your palms.Add the mutton mince to the mixing bowl.
Add all other ingredients except for oil and bread crumbs into the bowl including ginger, garlic, onion, coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chillies, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, potato, salt, cloves and cinnamon stick.
Mix well.Take lemon sized ball of mutton mix and roll it well.
Now gently flatten it to form a disc.Do this step for rest of the mutton mix.
Coat the cutlet with bread crumbs and keep aside.Heat oil in a shallow pan for shallow frying.
Once oil is hot, place a few cutlets on the pan and fry on medium-low heat until browned on all sides and mutton is fully cooked.Do this on medium low heat as we want the mutton to get cooked through the inside too.
I coated my cutlets on bread and suji mix just to add some texture over the cutlet.Once cooked, remove from pan and set it on kitchen paper.Serve Parsi Mutton Cutlets Recipe along with Dhaniya Pudina Chutney, Date Tamarind Chutney or any other chutney of your choice and Pickled Onions.
generated_text:
Parsi Mutton Cutlets Recipe

Ingredients:

- 500g boneless mutton, cut into small pieces
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp garlic paste
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 tbsp butter
- 1 tbsp chopped coriander
- Lemon wedges, for serving

Instructions:

1. In a large pan, heat the oil and butter over medium heat.

2. Add the mutton pieces and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until they are browned on all sides.

3. Add the onion, green chilies, ginger paste, garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt. Mix well to combine.

4. Cover the pan and cook for another 10-15 minutes, or until the mutton is tender and the spices are well-cooked.

5. Remove the lid and let the excess oil drain off.

6. Form the mutton mixture into small balls or patties.

7. Heat a frying pan over medium heat and add a little oil.

8. Fry the cutlets for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.

9. Serve the Parsi mutton cutlets hot with a side of steamed rice and a wedge of lemon.

Enjoy this delicious and hearty Parsi dish with your loved ones!

user_message: Generate a Bengali Recipes recipe for Phalguni Dal Recipe - Phalguni Dal Recipe
reference_text:
**Phalguni Dal Recipe - Phalguni Dal Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 tsp rye, 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds, 2 tsp mustard oil, 2 cloves garlic - grated, 1 onion - finely chopped,1/4 cup lentils - wash, asafoetida - a pinch, 1 tsp garam masala powder, 1/4 tsp Kalonji seeds, 1/2 inch ginger - grated, salt - as per taste, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 2 green chillies - Cut, 1/2 tsp fennel, 1/4 cup green peas, 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tomato - finely chopped, 1/2 tsp fennel seeds

**Instructions:**
To make Falguni Dal recipe, first wash the lentil lentils thoroughly.
Now put lentils, tomatoes, onions, ginger, garlic, 1-1 / 2 cups of water in a pressure cooker and turn off the cooker.
Turn on the gas and cook till 3 cities come.
Turn off the gas and let the pressure drain automatically.
Open the cooker and mash the lentils.
Keep it separately.
Now heat 2 tsp mustard oil in a pan for tempering.
Add rai, fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, cumin, kalonji and cook till the cumin seeds crackle.
Now add green chilies and cook for 1 minute.
Add asafetida, turmeric powder and green peas, cover the pan and cook on medium heat for 2 minutes.
After 2 minutes add masoor dal, garam masala, salt and mix well.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
Garnish with green coriander and serve.
Serve Phalguni Dal with Bhindi Masala, Bundi Raita and Phulka for a day's dinner.

reference_text:
**Palak Paneer Bhurji Recipe -Spiced Cottage Cheese Scramble With Spinach Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Sunflower Oil - for cooking,2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,1 Tomato - finely chopped,50 grams Spinach Leaves (Palak) - finely chopped,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1 Onion - finely chopped,2 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Butter,200 grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) - crumbled,2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder)

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Tamil Nadu main course recipe for Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal-South Indian Style Yam Masiyal
reference_text:
**Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal-South Indian Style Yam Masiyal**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,Salt - to taste,1/2 teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split),2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon SSP Asafoetida (Hing),1/2 cup Raw Peanuts (Moongphali),2 sprig Curry leaves - torn,1/4 cup Tamarind Water,1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),500 grams Elephant yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu),1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 tablespoon Sesame (Gingelly) Oil

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal, wash and clean the yam, peel the skin and cut them into 1 inch cubes.
Transfer these yam cubes into the pressure cooker along with tamarind water, turmeric powder and salt to taste.Close the lid and pressure cook for about 4 whistles.
Turn off the heat and allow the pressure to release naturally.
Once the pressure has released naturally open the cooker and mash the yam using a masher, leave a few yam chunks to add to the texture of the Chenai Masiyal.
Next cook the raw peanuts.
Add the raw peanuts into a pressure cooker along with 1/2 cup of water and salt to taste.Close the lid and pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles.
Allow the pressure to release naturally and keep aside.Now we will continue to make the Chenai Kizhangu MasiyalHeat a pan on medium heat, add oil.
Once the oil is hot, add the mustard seeds and urad dal.
Allow the mustard seeds to crackle and the urad dal to turn golden brown.Once done add the hing, curry leaves, green chillies and saute for a few seconds.
Now add the cooked and mashed yam, turmeric powder, red chillie powder, salt to taste and cooked peanuts.
Saute for a few minutes until well combined.
Turn off the heat and transfer the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal into a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal along with Steamed Rice, Chettinad Style Poondu Rasam Recipe and Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari Recipe- With Pomegranates  for a complete meal.

Now add the cooked and mashed yam, turmeric powder, red chillie powder, salt to taste and cooked peanuts.
Saute for a few minutes until well combined.
Turn off the heat and transfer the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal into a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal along with Steamed Rice, Chettinad Style Poondu Rasam Recipe and Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari Recipe- With Pomegranates  for a complete meal.

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Gajar Halwa Barfi Recipe (Carrot Fudge)
reference_text:
**Gajar Halwa Barfi Recipe (Carrot Fudge)**


**Ingredients:**
300 grams Carrot (Gajjar) - peeled and grated,200 grams Khoya (Mawa),1 cup Sugar,1 cup Milk,Saffron strands - a few,3 tablespoons Ghee,2 tablespoons Fresh cream,Dry Fruits - for garnsihing

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Gajar Halwa Barfi recipe, in a medium sized non-stick pan, add grated carrot and cook for 5 minutes.Add milk, saffron milk and mix it well.
Put the lid on top and cook for about 3 to 4 minutes.
Now once milk gets evaporated, add sugar, cream and stir it continually for 5 minutes.Once it gets dry, add half khoya/mava (100 grams) and again mix it well and cook for 3-4 minutes.Your Halwa layer is ready, take it out into greased tart plate or any other plate with ghee.
And gently press it using your palm once it gets warm.Place the tart plate into refrigerator for 10-15 minutes, meanwhile start preparation of khoya layer.
Into another medium sized non-stick pan, add ghee, once melted, add khoya and mix it well.Once it gets sticky, add remaining​ sugar into it and mix it well.
Switch off the gas and spread this khoya layer on the carrot halwa layer you prepared earlier and again press it gently using your palm.Garnish it with dry fruits of your choice and cut it in desired shapes and serve it to your loved ones on this Diwali!Serve this Gajar Halwa Barfi as a dessert after your festive meal of Dum Aloo, Puri and Boondi raita.
generated_text:
Ingredients:

- 1 kg carrots, grated
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup ghee
- 1/2 cup cashews, chopped
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder
- 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg powder
- 1/2 teaspoon saffron strands
- 1/2 teaspoon green cardamom powder
- 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg powder
- 1/2 teaspoon saffron strands

Instructions:

1. In a pan, heat the grated carrots with milk and water on low flame for 10 minutes.

2. Add sugar and cook on low flame until the carrots are soft and the liquid has reduced to a syrup consistency.

3. Add ghee and cook on low flame for another 5 minutes.

4. Add the chopped cashews and raisins and cook for another 2 minutes.

5. Add cardamom powder, nutmeg powder, and saffron strands. Mix well.

6. Grease a square pan with ghee and pour the carrot mixture into it.

7. Sprinkle the remaining ghee on top and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.

8. Once the carrot mixture has set, cut it into small squares and serve chilled.

Enjoy your delicious Gajar Halwa Barfi Recipe (Carrot Fudge) with your loved ones!

reference_text:
**Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoons Ginger - grated,Salt - to taste,Sunflower Oil - for making dosa,2 cups Carrots (Gajjar) - grated,4 cups Idli Dosa Batter

**Instructions:**
To begin making Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa Recipe, grate the carrots and ginger and keep it ready.Keep the Idli Dosa Batter ready as well.
Heat a teaspoon of oil in a pan, add the grated carrot and ginger, saute for a mint till it becomes a little softer.Once cool, grind it to a fine puree using a mixer grinder and pour this mixture into the bowl of dosa batter.
Season with salt and whisk the Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa batter well.
Now, to make the dosa, heat a skillet, brush it a little oil.
Pour a laddle full of the prepared batter and spread it to make a thin dosa.
Let it cook on the bottom for a minute.Flip over and cook the other side for another minute and take it out in a serving plate.Likewise, prepare the rest of the dosas with the remaining batter.Serve Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa with Sweet and Spicy Tomato Chutney Recipe and Beetroot Sambar for a complete breakfast.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian indian breakfast recipe for Ajwain Puri Recipe - Puri Flavoured With Carom Seeds
reference_text:
**Ajwain Puri Recipe - Puri Flavoured With Carom Seeds**


**Ingredients:**
Sunflower Oil,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Ajwain (Carom seeds),250 grams Whole Wheat Flour,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Ajwain puri Recipe by mixing all the ingredients includinh whole wheat flour, carom seeds, turmeric powder, salt with enough water in a bowl.Add sufficient water and knead in order to get a consistency of a stiff dough.
At the end add 2 tablespoon of oil to bind.Make equal small balls and roll them on to a board using a rolling pin.The thickness of the dough must not be too thin.Heat the oil in a Kadai with enough oil to fry the puris.After 5 to 10 minutes you can add a tiny bit of the dough into the oil to check if it is done.
If the dough rises up quickly then it is done.Now gently and carefully slide the puris into the oil.
Once it comes on top, with your ladel press the puri down so that it helps in puffing up.
Then later turn the puri and cook on both the side till you get a golden brown colour.Once done, take out on a kitchen towel to absorb the excess oil.
Serve your delicious puffed up Ajwain Puris with Amritsari Aloo or Palak Makhana and Boondi Raita to make it a complete meal.

Ajwain Puri Recipe - Puri Flavoured With Carom Seeds


Ingredients:

- 2 cups of whole wheat flour (atta)

- 1/2 teaspoon ajwain (carom seeds)

- 1/2 teaspoon salt

- 1/2 teaspoon sugar

- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder

- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda

- 1/2 cup warm water

- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil


1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the whole wheat flour, ajwain, salt, sugar, baking powder, and baking soda.

2. Add the warm water gradually to the dry ingredients, mixing continuously until a soft dough forms.

3. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15 minutes.

4. Divide the dough into small balls, slightly flatten them, and then roll them into circles.

5. Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat and place a rolled dough on it.

6. Cook the puri until it puffs up and turns golden brown on both sides. This should take about 2-3 minutes per side.

7. Once cooked, remove the puri from the pan and place it on a serving plate.

8. Serve the Ajwain Puri hot with a side of chutney or pickle for a delightful Indian breakfast.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe
reference_text:
**Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Water - for kneading,1 Green Chilli,1 cup Sweet corn,1/2 teaspoon Salt - for the dough,1 Onion - chopped,4 Potato (Aloo) - boiled,2 teaspoons Mango pickle - or any pickle of your choice,3 tablespoons Sunflower Oil - for rolling and frying,2 cups Whole Wheat Flour,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - a handful chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Corn Stuffed Paratha, we have to first wash the corn and keep the potatoes cooked.
You can either pressure cook the potatoes for 2 whistles or add them to saucepan with water and boil them for 15 mins/until it is cooked.Once the potatoes are cooked, peel the skin and add them to a bowl and mash them.
Once mashed, add chopped coriander leaves, sweet corn and homemade tomato pickle.
Mix well.
Add required amount of salt and mix the mixture well.
Keep aside.
The corn stuffing is ready.
Now we have to make the dough for the paratha.
In a flat bowl add wheat flour, salt and mix.
Add water little by little and knead the dough until it is smooth and homogeneously mixed.
Apply a coating of oil on top of the ball of dough and leave it to rest for 10 minutes.Next, divide the dough into medium sized balls.
Take one ball of dough and roll it out until its about 4-5 inches diameter.
 Place one tablespoon of stuffing in the center and join the ends of the paratha dough so that the stuffing in encased in the dough.
Do the same with the rest of the dough and corn mixture.
Place a tawa on medium heat and add some oil.
Place the stuffed paratha on the tawa and cook on both sides until done.
Drizzle some oil on top of the paratha when cooking on both sides.
Take off the pan and finish the rest of the rolled out parathas.
Serve Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe with Pisarna Manga Recipe and Homemade Curd

reference_text:
**Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi Recipe (Saffron Bread Pudding With Spiced Milk Custard)**


**Ingredients:**
1/3 cup Water,Saffron strands - few,1/3 cup Sugar,6 Whole Wheat Brown Bread,1/4 cup Ghee - (for shallow frying),1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 cup Rabri,Mixed nuts - Chopped (almonds

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi Recipe, we will first prepare the sugar syrup for the Shahi Tukda and Rabri.Prepare the Rabri according to the Rabri recipe and keep aside.Heat water and sugar in pan, add the saffron strands and allow the water to come to a brisk boil.
Turn the heat to low and simmer the sugar water until you get a one string consistency.
Turn off the heat and keep the sugar syrup aside.The next step is to prepare the bread slices and roast them in ghee for the shahi tukda recipe.Cut the edges of the bread and slice them into a triangle.Heat ghee in a skillet and shallow try the bread pieces until they are crisp and golden brown in color on both sides.Once the bread slices are fried, soak each slice in the sugar syrup for about a minute and place them on a serving platter.
Pour the prepared Rabri over the bread slices and garnish with the chopped nuts and serve.You can serve the Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi either chilled or warm based on the season and mood.

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Peas Pulao Recipe -  North Indian Matar Pulao
reference_text:
**Peas Pulao Recipe -  North Indian Matar Pulao**


**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - sliced,1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,1/2 cup Green peas (Matar),1 tablespoon Ghee,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),2 Cloves (Laung),1-1/2 cup Basmati rice,1 Star anise,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Peas Pulao Recipe, wash the rice and soak in water for about 20 minutes.
Heat a pressure cooker with ghee on medium flame, to this add the whole spices - bay leaf, cumin seeds, star anise, cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon stick.
Once these spices begin to sizzle, add the sliced onions, and cook them only until they begin to sweat.
This will take about a minute.
To this add the salt, green peas and drained rice.
Top the matar pulao with about 2-1/2 of water, depending on the age and quality of your rice and close the pressure cooker.
Pressure cook for about 3 whistles and turn off the flame, allow the pressure to release naturally.
Once done, open the pressure cooker give the Matar pulao a stir and fluff up the rice.Transfer the Matar Pulao to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Peas Pulao Recipe along with Smoked Dhaba Dal Recipe, Masala Lachha Paratha Recipe, Achari Paneer Masala Recipe or Easy Creamy Chicken Curry Recipe, Pickled Onions and finish the meal with a dessert of Rose Gulkand Phirni Recipe.
generated_text:
Ingredients:

- 1 cup basmati rice
- 1/2 cup green peas
- 1/2 cup chopped onions
- 2 tablespoons ghee or oil
- 1 teaspoon cumin seeds
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard seeds
- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
- 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder
- 1/2 teaspoon garam masala
- Salt to taste
- 2 cups water

Instructions:

1. Rinse the basmati rice in cold water until the water runs clear. Drain the rice and set aside.

2. In a large pot, heat the ghee or oil over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and mustard seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.

3. Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent.

4. Add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, and garam masala. Stir well to combine.

5. Add the drained rice and mix well to coat the grains with the spices.

6. Add the green peas and salt to taste. Mix well.

7. Add the water to the pot and bring it to a boil.

8. Once the water is boiling, reduce the heat to low and cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid.

9. Simmer the rice for about 15-20 minutes or until the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked through.

10. Remove the pot from the heat and let it sit covered for 5 minutes to allow the rice to steam.

11. Fluff the rice with a fork and serve hot with a side of raita or any Indian side dish of your choice.
