user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipes snack recipe for Maharashtrian Dinkache Ladoo Recipe (Whole Wheat Flour Truffles With Edible Gum & Dry Fruits)
reference_text:
**Maharashtrian Dinkache Ladoo Recipe (Whole Wheat Flour Truffles With Edible Gum & Dry Fruits)**


**Ingredients:**
1-1/2 cups Sugar - or 1 cup powdered Jaggery,1 teaspoon Dry ginger powder,1 cup Ghee,1/2 cup Gond (natural gum),1/2 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),3 cups Whole Wheat Flour,1 cup White Urad Dal (Split),1 cup Dry dates powder (kharik pud),1/2 teaspoon Nutmeg powder,1 cup Mixed nuts - (Almonds,1 cup Dessicated Coconut

**Instructions:**
To begin making Maharashtrian Dinkache Ladoo Recipe (Whole Wheat Flour Truffles With Edible Gum & Dry Fruits), heat a heavy bottom pan and dry roast the split and skinned black gram on a low to medium flame for 7-10 minutes.Keep stirring the dal to ensure it is been roasted evenly and it does not catch the bottom of the pan.
You will get a nice aroma while you roast the dal.
Remove the roasted dal in a plate.
On cooling, grind to a fine powder using a grinder.
Grate the dry coconut using grater and keep aside.
Take edible gum in a grinder jar and make a fine powder.
Similarly powder the sugar and keep aside.Dry roast the dry fruits on low to medium flame for about 3- 5 minutes.
On cooling coarsely grind them or chop them.Heat the same heavy bottom large wok , add 1/2 cup ghee and to it add the powdered urad dal .Fry on low flame until pink in color.
This may take around 5 minutes.
   Now add the whole wheat flour to the roasted urad dal in batches and keep stirring continuously.
Roast the wheat flour for about 4-5 minutes without adding more ghee.To the roasted urad dal and wheat flour, add the powdered edible gum.
Roast for 2-3 minutes.Now add the powdered dried dates.
Roast for 1-2 minutes.(In many Indian groceries, you can get powdered dry dates easily .
In case you do not find, it can be made at home.
De seed the dried dates ( chuara/ Kharik ) .
Now cut into small pieces using a sharp knife or scissors.
Put the chopped pieces of dried dates into a grinder jar or food processor to get a coarse powder )Now add grated dried coconut followed by the chopped nuts to the mixture and keep stirring the entire mixture using a large ladle.Now put off the heat and add the powdered sugar followed by the dry ginger powder and the sweet masala.
Blend everything very well.
If not making the ladoos as offering to God, you can taste the ladoo mixture at this stage.Now our ladoo mixture is ready.
Add the remaining melted 1/2 cup ghee to it as needed.
 Mix well and check by taking handful of mixture in your palms, if you are able to bind ladoos, do not add more ghee.
If you are not able to form  a ball shape then you can add all the remaining ghee.
Roll between the palm to give a round shape.Make all the ladoos similarly while still warm.
As once the mixture cools down, then the ghee solidifies making it difficult to bind the ladoos.
This happens especially in the area where the temperature is low.
On cooling keep the ladoos in air tight containers and store at room temperature.
Serve Maharashtrian Dinkache Ladoo Recipe (Whole Wheat Flour Truffles With Edible Gum & Dry Fruits) with Badam Kheer Recipe (Spiced Almond Milk) or Turmeric Milk Recipe (Haldi Doodh) for a winter breakfast.
generated_text:
**Maharashtrian Dinkache Ladoo Recipe (Whole Wheat Flour Truffles With Edible Gum & Dry Fruits)**


**Ingredients:**

- 2 cups of whole wheat flour (Atta)

- 1/2 cup of edible gum (Gond)

- 1/2 cup of mixed dry fruits (cashews, almonds, and raisins)

- 1/2 cup of sugar

- 1/2 cup of water

- 1/2 teaspoon of cardamom powder

- A pinch of saffron (optional)

- Ghee (clarified butter) for coating


**Instructions:**


1. Heat a pan and add the whole wheat flour. Stir continuously to prevent the flour from sticking to the pan.

2. Once the flour turns golden brown, add the edible gum and dry fruits. Stir well to ensure that the gum and fruits are coated with the flour.

3. Add the sugar and water to the mixture. Stir until the sugar dissolves completely.

4. Add the cardamom powder and saffron (if using) to the mixture. Stir well to combine all the ingredients.

5. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool down for a few minutes.

6. Once coo0led, form small balls with the mixture. Roll each ball in ghee and then in whole wheat flour to prevent sticking.

7. Place the formed truffles on a greased baking sheet and let them cool completely.

8. Serve the Maharashtrian Dinkache Ladoo at room temperature or chilled.


Enjoy these delightful Maharashtrian snacks with your loved ones!

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Bhindi Masala Gravy Recipe - Ladies Finger In Tomato Onion Gravy
reference_text:
**Bhindi Masala Gravy Recipe - Ladies Finger In Tomato Onion Gravy**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,5 cloves Garlic,1 tablespoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves),1 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 inch Ginger,250 grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) - thinly sliced,2 tablespoons Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),2 Green Chillies - chopped,2 Cloves (Laung),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 Mace (Javitri),1 Onion - roughly chopped,Sunflower Oil,3 Tomatoes - roughly chopped,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Bhindi Masala Gravy Recipe, make sure to first rinse and dry each Bhindi.
This process is done in order to ensure that the bhindi doesn't get sticky while cooking it.
After wiping the bhindis dry, slit the bhindi vertically till about half an inch from the tail (thinly sliced), so that each bhindi is slit but held together by the tail.
Heat oil for cooking into a pan; add the bhindi, sprinkle some salt and saute the bhindi until cooked.Cover the pan of the bhindi partially while it is cooking.
This will help the bhindi steam cook, at the same time roast a bit.
Keeping the lip ajar, helps the steam at the same time, trap a little steam.
It will take a good 10 minutes to cook the bhindi.
Keep stirring to ensure it gets roasted well on all sides.Next step is to make the gravy for the Bhindi Masala.Add the tomatoes, into a mixer grinder and make a puree.
Keep aside.In a mixer grinder, add garlic, onions, ginger, chillies, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon stick and made and grind it to a smooth paste.
Keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat; add in the bay leaf and saute for about 10 seconds.After 10 seconds, add in the  onions garlic and spice paste and saute for 2 to 3 minutes.Add the turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder and mix everything well.
Cook for about a minute until the raw smell goes away.
Next, add the tomato puree, curd and salt.
It will take about 2 to 3 minutes over medium heat for the tomato to cook.
Check the salt and allow the masala to be thick.Once done, add the roasted bhindi into the masala gravy.
Check the salt and turn the heat to high and cook for another minute so that the bhindi gets coated well with the masala.After a couple of minutes, add the crushed kasuri methi and turn off the heat.Garnish the Bhindi Masala Gravy with chopped coriander leaves and serve hot.Serve Bhindi Masala Gravy along with Paneer Biryani, Jackfruit Biryani or any Biryani of your choice for a vegetarian lunch or dinner.
If can also serve the along with Tawa Paratha and Boondi Raita for an Indian lunch or dinner.
generated_text:
Bhindi Masala Gravy Recipe - Ladies Finger In Tomato Onion Gravy

Ingredients:

- 500 grams of fresh okra (ladies finger)
- 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil
- 1 large onion, finely chopped
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds
- 1 teaspoon of coriander seeds
- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder
- 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder
- 1/2 teaspoon of garam masala
- Salt to taste
- 1/2 cup of finely chopped fresh coriander (cilantro)
- 1/2 cup of water

Instructions:

1. Wash the okra thoroughly and pat dry with a kitchen towel. Cut off the ends and slice them into 1-inch pieces.

2. Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and fry for a few seconds until they start to crackle.

3. Add the finely chopped onions and green chilies to the pan. Fry until the onions turn translucent and start to brown.

4. Add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, and garam masala to the pan. Stir well to combine.

5. Add the sliced okra to the pan and mix well with the spices.

6. Cover the pan and cook on low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally. The okra should be soft and the excess moisture should have evaporated.

7. Add salt to taste and the finely chopped coriander. Mix well.

8. Add 1/2 cup of water and simmer for another 5 minutes until the gravy thickens slightly.

9. Serve hot with rice or roti.

Enjoy your delicious Bhindi Masala Gravy!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Coastal Karnataka lunch recipe for Karwar Style Valval Recipe (Mixed Vegetables In Coconut Milk)
reference_text:
**Karwar Style Valval Recipe (Mixed Vegetables In Coconut Milk)**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Green beans (French Beans) - Chopped,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1/2 Mangalorean cucumber - chopped into small cubes,2 Dry Red Chilli,1 Carrot (Gajjar) - chopped into small cubes,6 Curry leaves,1/2 Green peas (Matar) - chopped into small cubes,5 Green Chillies - slit,1 tablespoon Coconut Oil,1-1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),3 cups Coconut milk,1 Potato (Aloo) - chopped into small cubes,1 cup Pointed gourd (Parval) - slit

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Karwar Style Valval Recipe (Mixed Vegetables In Coconut Milk), boil all the vegetables - beans, carrot, peas, potato, mangalore cucumber, parval and green chilies in water.
They should be done but not mushy, so don't pressure cook.Add the coconut milk, salt and cook for another 5 to 6 minutes.
Take off the heat.The next step is to prepare the tempering.
Heat little coconut oil in a heavy bottomed pan.Add mustard and cumin seeds.
When they start popping, add the red chilies and curry leaves.Pour the seasoning in the gravy and mix.
Serve hot.
 Serve Karwar Style Valval Recipe (Mixed Vegetables In Coconut Milk) along with Whole Wheat Lachha Paratha and Beetroot Pachadi for a weekday meal.
generated_text:
Karwar Style Valval Recipe (Mixed Vegetables in Coconut Milk)


Ingredients:

- 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrots, potatoes, green beans, and cauliflower)

- 1/2 cup grated coconut

- 1/2 cup fresh coconut milk

- 1/2 teaspoon mustard seeds

- 1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds

- 1/4 teaspoon asafoetida (hing)

- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder

- 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder

- 1/2 teaspoon salt

- 1 tablespoon oil

- Fresh coriander leaves for garnish


1. Wash the mixed vegetables thoroughly and cut them into bite-sized pieces.

2. Heat the oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and asafoetida. Once the seeds start to crackle, add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt. Stir well for a few seconds.

3. Add the cut vegetables to the pressure cooker and mix well with the spices.

4. Pour in the coconut milk and add enough water to cover the vegetables.

5. Close the pressure cooker with the lid and cook on high heat for 3 whistles.

6. After the pressure cooker cools down, open the lid and check if the vegetables are cooked. If not, cook for a few more whistles.

7. In a small pan, heat the grated coconut over low heat until it turns golden brown.

8. Add the toasted coconut to the pressure cooker and mix well.

9. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves before serving.


Serve the Karwar Style Valval hot with rice or Indian flatbread (roti or chapati).

reference_text:
**Green Apple Rasam Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 Dry Red Chilli,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),3/4 cup Arhar dal (Split Toor Dal) - cooked,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1 Tomato,Water - Water,Ghee - as required,Curry leaves - Few,1 Green apple,1/2 tablespoon Rasam Powder,2 cloves Garlic - finely chopped (optional),1/2 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),4 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Green Apple Rasam, firstly cook dal,  by adding it in the pressure cooker along with 1/2 a cup of water.
Close the pressure cooker and pressure cook the dal for 4 whistles.
Turn off the flame, allowing the pressure to release naturally.
Drain the water and keep it aside.Chop green apple, tomato and blend them into a juice by adding water in a mixer grinder.
Do not strain.
Take a heavy bottomed pan, add the apple tomato juice, hing, curry leaves, turmeric and salt.
Bring it to a boil.Add rasam powder and dal and bring it to a boil.
Once it is done, now we will prepare the tempering.Heat ghee in a tadka pan on medium flame, add the mustard seeds, allow it to splutter cumin seeds, garlic, and dry chilliPour it into the rasam, garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.Serve Green Apple Rasam along with hot steamed rice, Elai Vadam, Raw Jackfruit Poriyal Recipe - South Indian Style Stir Fry and Spiced Butter Milk With Coriander And Ginger Recipe for a perfect meal.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Spinach Paneer Kofta Curry Recipe
reference_text:
**Spinach Paneer Kofta Curry Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
5 cups Spinach Leaves (Palak) - chopped,Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves) - for flavour and garnish,1 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),1/4 cup Gram flour (besan),Salt - to taste,1 inch Ginger - grated,1/2 cup Whole Wheat Bread crumbs,1/2 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),Red Chilli powder - to taste,3-4 cloves Garlic - grated,Sunflower Oil - for pan frying,1/2 cup Fresh cream,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder - 1 teaspoon of homemade garam masala,1 tablespoon Honey,1 cup Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) - crumbled

**Instructions:**
Method for KoftasHeat 1 teaspoon of oil in a pan; sauté the spinach on high heat until it gets soft enough and until all the moisture evaporates.
Turn off heat at this point.Allow the spinach to cool completely.
Once the spinach cools, add the gram flour and salt to taste and combine well.
Divide the spinach into 8 equal portions.Mix paneer, cumin powder and salt; divide into 8 balls and keep aside.Take a portion of the spinach, flatten it on your palm and stuff the paneer balls in it.
Shape it into a ball (kofta).
While doing this, you might feel that the spinach mixture is not that pliable that it easily folds onto the paneer and makes a ball.
That's the right feeling.
It's not very easy to handle them.
If you feel the spinach does not cover the paneer properly, just patch the paneer all around with the spinach mixture.
It works!Roll each ball in over the breadcrumbs so they get coated evenly.
You can now cook the kofta's in the Oven or in the Kuzhipaniyaram PanIf you plan to use the Oven to bake the kofta's; then place the kofta balls on a greased baking sheet.
Brush the kofta balls with some oil and bake in a preheated oven (200 C) until the koftas turn golden brown in colour.
Remove the kofta's from the oven once baked and keep them aside.If you plan to use the Kuzhipaniyaram Pan; then place the kofta balls in the pan's cavities; drizzle the kofta's with a little oil and pan fry them until golden brown on both sides.

reference_text:
**Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1-1/2 cup Instant Oats (Oatmeal),1 Carrot (Gajjar) - grated,1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,Salt - to taste,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - a bunch,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Cashew nuts - broken,1/2 teaspoon Enos fruit salt,1 sprig Curry leaves - finely chopped,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1 tablespoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1/2 cup Sooji (Semolina/ Rava)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe, we will first prepare the tempering/tadka that will be added to the Oats Rava Idli.Heat oil in a skillet on medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and as they begin to splutter, add chana dal and roast the dal until it turns golden brown.
Once the dal has turned golden brown, add chopped green chilies, curry leaves and asafoetida and saute for a few seconds.Turn off the flame and set aside to cool.
In another pan, on medium flame dry roast the oats until you get the roasted aroma and it slightly changes in color.
Once roasted, allow it to cool and blend to a make a powder and keep it aside.In a large mixing bowl, add the oats powder and roasted rava, roasted seasoning ingredients, grated carrots and chopped coriander leaves.
Mix well.Add yogurt, salt and little water at a time to make it to a thick batter.
The consistency of the oats idli batter should be slightly loose than a idli batter.
Set it aside for 10-15 mins.
The batter will thicken while it rests.
After the resting period, add the enos fruit salt and mix again.Preheat an idli steamer with water.
Grease idli plates with oil and pour the batter into each cavity of the idli plate.
Place the Oats Rava Idli into the steamer and steam for 10-15 minutes until cooked through.
Serve the Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe along with some Tomato Onion Chutney or Vengayam Sambar for a weeknight dinner.

Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe

Ingredients:

- 1 cup instant oats
- 1 cup rice flour
- 1/2 cup urad dal (split black gram)
- 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds (methi)
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1.5 cups water

Instructions:

1. Soak the urad dal and fenugreek seeds in water for 4-6 hours.

2. Drain the water and grind the urad dal and fenugreek seeds into a fine paste.

3. In a mixing bowl, combine the instant oats, rice flour, urad dal paste, salt, and baking soda. Mix well.

4. Add water gradually and mix until a thick batter is formed.

5. Cover the batter and let it rest for 3-4 hours.

6. After resting, check the consistency of the batter. If it's too thick, add a little water. If it's too thin, add a little more oats or rice flour.

7. Heat a non-stick idli pan or a steamer.

8. Grease the idli moulds with a little oil.

9. Pour a ladleful of batter into each mould and steam for 10-12 minutes or until the idlis are cooked and springy to touch.

10. Serve hot with sambar or coconut chutney.

Enjoy your Instant Oats Rava Idli!
ref_name: Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe
ref_instructions:
To begin making the Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe, we will first prepare the tempering/tadka that will be added to the Oats Rava Idli.Heat oil in a skillet on medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and as they begin to splutter, add chana dal and roast the dal until it turns golden brown.
Once the dal has turned golden brown, add chopped green chilies, curry leaves and asafoetida and saute for a few seconds.Turn off the flame and set aside to cool.
In another pan, on medium flame dry roast the oats until you get the roasted aroma and it slightly changes in color.
Once roasted, allow it to cool and blend to a make a powder and keep it aside.In a large mixing bowl, add the oats powder and roasted rava, roasted seasoning ingredients, grated carrots and chopped coriander leaves.
Mix well.Add yogurt, salt and little water at a time to make it to a thick batter.
The consistency of the oats idli batter should be slightly loose than a idli batter.
Set it aside for 10-15 mins.
The batter will thicken while it rests.
After the resting period, add the enos fruit salt and mix again.Preheat an idli steamer with water.
Grease idli plates with oil and pour the batter into each cavity of the idli plate.
Place the Oats Rava Idli into the steamer and steam for 10-15 minutes until cooked through.
Serve the Instant Oats Rava Idli Recipe along with some Tomato Onion Chutney or Vengayam Sambar for a weeknight dinner.
gen_instructions:
1. Soak the urad dal and fenugreek seeds in water for 4-6 hours.

2. Drain the water and grind the urad dal and fenugreek seeds into a fine paste.

3. In a mixing bowl, combine the instant oats, rice flour, urad dal paste, salt, and baking soda. Mix well.

4. Add water gradually and mix until a thick batter is formed.

5. Cover the batter and let it rest for 3-4 hours.

6. After resting, check the consistency of the batter. If it's too thick, add a little water. If it's too thin, add a little more oats or rice flour.

7. Heat a non-stick idli pan or a steamer.

8. Grease the idli moulds with a little oil.

10. Serve hot with sambar or coconut chutney.

Enjoy your Instant Oats Rava Idli!

user_message: Generate an Indo Chinese recipe for Cheesy Chinese Bhel Recipe
reference_text:
**Cheesy Chinese Bhel Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Spring Onion Greens - a few,1 Carrot (Gajjar) - thinly sliced,2 cloves Garlic,150 grams Hakka Noodles,1 tablespoon Britannia Cheese Spread - Roasted Garlic,2 inch Ginger - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali),1 tablespoon Red Chilli sauce,1 Onion - thinly sliced,1 Green Chilli,2 tablespoons Red Chilli sauce,1 tablespoon Soy sauce,Salt - to taste,1 cup Green beans - thinly sliced,1 Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) - thinly sliced,1/4 cup Britannia Cheese Block - grated,1 cup Mint Leaves (Pudina),4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1 teaspoon Sugar,1 cup Cabbage (Patta Gobi/ Muttaikose) - thinly sliced,1 inch Ginger,2 tablespoons Sweet Chutney (Date & Tamarind),1/4 cup Water

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Cheesy Chinese Bhel Recipe, we will first make the crispy noodles.Into hot boiling water, add the hakka noodles and salt to taste.
Boil the noodles until the noodles soften but is not fully cooked.
Once the noodles are half cooked, drain the water and wash it under cold water to prevent more cooking.Now we will deep fry the noodles.
Into preheated oil, add the half cooked noodles and deep fry until golden brown and crisp.
Keep aside.
To make the Cheesy Pudina Chutney, into a blender add the pudina, garlic, green chillies, ginger, roasted peanuts, sugar, salt to taste, Britannia Roasted Garlic Cheese Spreadz and water.
Blend them all to make a smooth chutney.
Keep aside.To Make the Roasted VegetablesInto a preheated kadai add oil, garlic, ginger, onions and saute for a few seconds.
Add the capsicum, carrot, beans and cabbage, sprinkle some salt.
Stir fry on high heat for 2 to 3 minutes until the veggies are power cooked.They should have a crunch and yet feel cooked.
Finally stir in the soy sauce & the chilli sauce and stir.
Turn off the heat.
Keep aside.
The final step is to toss to make the chinese bhel.Into a large mixing bowl, add the noodles, the veggies, the cheesy pudina sauce and the sweet and spicy sauce.
Toss together until combined.
Add the grated Britannia cheese and some fresh spring onions and give it a mix.
Transfer the Cheesy Chinese bhel to small serving bowl and serve immediately.
 Serve Cheesy Chinese Bhel Recipe with a thirst quencher like Kiwi Raw Mango Mint Masala Sherbet in the evenings.

reference_text:
**Classic South Indian Rava Idli Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1 Carrot (Gajjar) - grated,2 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1 teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split),Salt - to taste,1 inch Ginger - grated,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Enos fruit salt,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1 sprig Curry leaves - finely chopped,2 tablespoons Cashew nuts - broken,1 cup Sooji (Semolina/ Rava)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Classic South indian Rava Idli Recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep them ready.Heat a teaspoon of oil in a pan on medium flame, add mustard seeds and allow it to crackle.
Add the torn curry leaves, grated ginger, green chilies and cashew nuts.
Roast them for a few minutes.
Add the Semolina/Rava and roast until you get a good aroma.
Set aside to cool.
Set aside the roasted rava idli mixture to cool.In a mixing bowl combine the roasted rava idli mixture along with the yogurt, and add just enough water to make a thick batter and keep aside to rest for 10 to 15 minutes.After the resting period, you will have to add a little water as the rava idli batter would have soaked up all the moisture.
Add the grated carrots and enos fruit salt and whisk the rava idli batter.If the batter is too thick add water to make a thick batter of dropping consistency.
Stir well for a couple of minutesPreheat an idli steamer with enough water and keep it  ready.Grease the idli mould with oil and spoon the Rava Idli mixture into the cavities.
Place it in a preheated steamer and steam for 10 minutes until done.When you test, the rava idli should be cooked and the tester should come out clean.Serve the Rava Idli along with South Indian Coconut Chutney and Potato Saagu for a quick and wholesome breakfast.
generated_text:
Classic South Indian Rava Idli Recipe


Ingredients:

- 1 cup idli rice (thinai)

- 1/2 cup split pearl millet (varagu)

- 1/2 cup urad dal (black gram)

- 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds (methi)

- 1 tsp salt

- Water as needed


1. Soak the idli rice, split pearl millet, and urad dal separately for 4-6 hours or overnight.

2. After soaking, grind the idli rice and urad dal together with the fenugreek seeds to a smooth batter. Ensure the batter is not too watery.

3. Add salt to the batter and mix well.

4. Heat a non-stick idli pan or a steamer.

5. Grease the idli moulds with a little oil.

6. Pour a ladleful of the batter into each mould and steam for 10-12 minutes or until the idlis are set and cooked through.

7. Once cooked, remove the idlis from the pan and let them cool for a few minutes.

8. Serve the idlis hot with sambar, coconut chutney, or any other South Indian accompaniments.


Enjoy your classic South Indian Rava Idli for a hearty and healthy breakfast!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipes indian breakfast recipe for Rava Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe (Maharashtrian Style Semolina-Cucumber Flatbread)
reference_text:
**Rava Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe (Maharashtrian Style Semolina-Cucumber Flatbread)**


**Ingredients:**
Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - a small bunch,Ghee - Ghee as required for cooking the thalipeeth (~ 2-3 tablespoons),Salt - to taste,3 Green Chillies - finely chopped or crushed (adjust to taste),2 Cucumbers - peeled and coarsely grated,1 cup Sooji (Semolina/ Rava),3 tablespoons Curd (Dahi / Yogurt)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Rava Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe (Maharashtrian Style Semolina-Cucumber Flatbread) first stir together semolina, cucumber, chillies, coriander leaves, curd and salt in a mixing bowl.Add water as required (about 2-3 tablespoon) to make a dough that just holds all the ingredients together.
The cucumber will also release some water as it mixes, so add water cautiously so that the dough doesn't lose stiffness.Cover the bowl and keep the dough in it aside for 15-20 minutes to rest.Add more water if required to make a pliable dough.Divide the dough in 4-5 portions.Grease a flat skillet with some ghee.Place 1 portion of the dough on the greased pan and pat to make a thin circle (about 6 inches)With your finger make one hole in the centre of the patted dough and 4 more round it.Drizzle few drops of ghee in these holes.Place the pan on low heat.
Cover and cook till the underside turns brown.Flip the Thalipeeth and cook for a minute or till done, on low heat.Repeat this procedure with the rest of the dough portions, patting the thalipeeth directly on the skillet.Serve Rava Kakdi Thalipeeth Recipe (Maharashtrian Style Semolina-Cucumber Flatbread) with your favourite Red Chilli Thecha (Maharashtrian style Red chilli chutney Recipe) or Green Coriander and Coconut Chutney Recipe and dahi (yogurt) for a comforting breakfast or a mini meal.
generated_text:
**Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipe: Rava Kakdi Thalipeeth**


**Ingredients:**

- 2 cups fine semolina (rava)

- 1 large cucumber, grated

- 1/2 cup finely chopped onions

- 1/2 cup finely chopped tomatoes

- 1/2 cup finely chopped coriander leaves

- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder

- 1 teaspoon red chili powder

- 1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)

- 1 teaspoon oil

- Water as needed


1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the fine semolina (rava) with grated cucumber, chopped onions, tomatoes, and coriander leaves.

2. Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt to the mixture. Mix well to ensure all the ingredients are evenly coated with the spices.

3. Gradually add water to the mixture, stirring continuously to avoid lumps. The consistency should be like that of a thick pancake batter.

4. Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a teaspoon of oil.

5. Once the oil is hot, pour a ladleful of the batter onto the center of the pan. Spread it out evenly by moving the ladle in a circular motion.

6. Cook for about 2-3 minutes or until the bottom side is golden brown and crispy.

7. Flip the kakdi thalipeeth carefully and cook the other side for another 2-3 minutes until it is also golden brown and crispy.

8. Repeat the process with the remaining batter, adding a little more oil to the pan if needed.

9. Serve hot with a side of yogurt or a chutney of your choice.


Enjoy your Maharashtrian-style Rava Kakdi Thalipeeth for a healthy and delicious vegetarian breakfast!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian lunch recipe for Sweet Potato And Bitter Gourd Kulambu Recipe
reference_text:
**Sweet Potato And Bitter Gourd Kulambu Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
4 Dry Red Chillies,Water - as required,1 tablespoon Raw Peanuts (Moongphali),1-1/2 tablespoon Sambar Powder,1/4 cup Onion - thinly sliced,1/2 cup Sweet Potatoes,1 cup Karela (Bitter Gourd/ Pavakkai) - chopped,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),Salt - as needed,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,2 teaspoons Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),2 teaspoons Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 Tamarind - lemon sized,1 tablespoon Dry coconut (kopra),Curry leaves - few,Sesame (Gingelly) Oil - as required

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Sweet Potato And Bitter Gourd Kulambu recipe, soak tamarind in water and squeeze the juice.Take a wok/kadai or a clay pot and add oil.
Temper with mustard seeds, bengal gram, methi seeds, peanuts, red chilli and dry coconut.
Add onions and fry them until translucent.
Add sweet potato and fry until they are half cooked.At this stage add bitter gourd pieces and cook for 2 minutes.
Add curry leaves, salt, turmeric powder, sambar powder & coriander powder.
Mix them well.After 2 minutes, add tamarind water and cook until the mix thickens.
Once it is done, switch off the heat.Sweet Potato Bitter Gourd Kulambu is ready to serve.
Serve Sweet Potato And Bitter Gourd Kulambu along with Steamed Rice, Chow Chow Thoran and Elai Vadam for a weekday meal.

**Sweet Potato And Bitter Gourd Kulambu Recipe**


Ingredients:

- 2 medium-sized sweet potatoes, peeled and diced

- 2 bitter gourds (bottle gourds), peeled and sliced

- 1 tbsp mustard seeds

- 1 tbsp urad dal (split black gram)

- 1 tbsp chana dal (split chickpeas)

- 1 tbsp tamarind paste

- 1 tbsp jaggery (or sugar as a substitute)

- 1 tsp turmeric powder

- 1 tsp red chili powder

- Salt to taste

- 1 tbsp oil

- Fresh coriander leaves for garnish


1. Heat the oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds and let them splutter.

2. Add the urad dal and chana dal, and sauté until they turn golden brown.

3. Add the tamarind paste, jaggery, turmeric powder, and red chili powder. Stir well to combine.

4. Add the diced sweet potatoes and sliced bitter gourds to the pressure cooker.

5. Add salt to taste and mix well.

6. Cover the pressure cooker and cook for 3 whistles on high heat.

7. Once the pressure is released, open the cooker and check if the vegetables are cooked through.

8. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves before serving.


Serve the Sweet Potato And Bitter Gourd Kulambu with rice or Indian flatbread (roti or paratha).

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Potato Paneer Curry Without Onion And Garlic Recipe (Vrat Wale Aloo Paneer)
reference_text:
**Potato Paneer Curry Without Onion And Garlic Recipe (Vrat Wale Aloo Paneer)**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - as required,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),2 inch Ginger - grated,8 Potatoes (Aloo) - cut into medium sized pieces,5 Tomatoes,250 grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) - cut into medium cubes,Sunflower Oil - as required,3 Green Chillies - finely chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Potato Paneer Curry Without Onion And Garlic recipe, in a blender, add the tomatoes and 2 inch ginger pieces along with the green chillies.
Make a coarse puree and set aside.In a pressure cooker, heat 2 tablespoon of vegetable oil and add 1 tablespoon of grated ginger.Saute the ginger until it releases its fragrance, about a minute.Then add the puree and saute for about 10 minutes or until most of the water has evaporated.Add 1 teaspoon of red chilli powder, coriander powder and salt.
Saute for another 5 minutes or until the water has completely evaporated.Add the potatoes next and saute for 2 min.
Then add 2 cups of water and the roasted cumin powder.Stir, close the lid and allow one whistle.
Turn off the flame and let the pressure diffuse on its own.(If cooking in a pot cook until the potatoes are fully cooked.)Open the lid and bring the curry to a boil, then add the paneer cubes.
Cook for 2 minutes and finish the dish with lots of fresh coriander.Serve Potato Paneer Curry Without Onion And Garlic along with Rajgira Puri for the fasting days.

Stuffed Ridge Gourd Recipe (Vegetarian North Indian Style)


Ingredients:

- 2 large ridge gourds

- 1 cup of cooked rice

- 1/2 cup of finely chopped onions

- 1/2 cup of finely chopped tomatoes

- 1/2 cup of finely chopped coriander leaves

- 1/2 cup of finely chopped green chilies

- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of garam masala

- Salt to taste

- 2 tablespoons of oil

- 1/2 cup of grated paneer (optional)


1. Wash the ridge gourds and cut them into halves lengthwise. Remove the seeds and the white pith.

2. In a pan, heat the oil and add the chopped onions. Sauté until they turn translucent.

3. Add the chopped tomatoes and green chilies to the pan. Cook until the tomatoes are soft and the oil starts to separate.

4. Add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, and garam masala. Mix well.

5. Add the cooked rice to the pan and mix it with the spices. Cook for a few minutes until the rice is well coated with the spices.

6. If you are using paneer, add it to the rice mixture and mix well.

7. Stuff the rice mixture into the hollowed-out ridge gourds.

8. Place the stuffed ridge gourds in a pressure cooker and cook for 3 whistles.

9. Once cooked, let the ridge gourds cool down for a few minutes.

10. Serve the stuffed ridge gourds hot with a side of yogurt and some lemon wedges.


Enjoy your delicious vegetarian North Indian stuffed ridge gourd lunch!

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly North Indian Recipes north indian breakfast recipe for Sweet Potato & Methi Stuffed Paratha Recipe
reference_text:
**Sweet Potato & Methi Stuffed Paratha Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 inch Ginger - chopped,Salt - to taste,2 Sweet Potato - skin peeled and boiled,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) - chopped,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),2 tablespoon Mustard oil,1 Green Chilli - chopped,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1 cup Whole Wheat Flour,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Sweet Potato & Methi Stuffed Paratha Recipe, pressure cook the sweet potato with water for about 4 whistle.
Release the pressure naturally.
Once they cool down, peel the potatoes and mash them.
Keep them aside.In the meantime clean the methi and keep it ready.
Wash and chop them roughly.Add wheat flour, salt, oil.
Knead it into soft dough by adding little water for the Chapati or the outer layer.
Keep it covered and leave it aside.Heat a sauce pan with mustard oil, add cumin seeds and allow it sizzle for few seconds.
Add in ginger and saute till they are translucent.Add chopped methi leaves and saute till the leaves are cooked.
Add mashed potato, green chillies and all the spice powders including turemric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder.Give it a mix and check for salt.
Leave it aside to cool down for a while.Divide the stuffing into 4 equal portions.
Divide the dough also into 4 equal portions, take one small ball of dough, Roll the dough ball into a small thick flat round shape.
Place the stuffing in the center and close from all sides.Roll it very carefully to a round shaped bread, with 1 centimeter thickness.
It can be little thick also.Now to cook the Paratha, first preheat the skillet on medium heat.
Place the rolled paratha on top and allow it to cook on one side.
After about 10 to 15 seconds, flip the paratha.At this stage, smear some oil or ghee to cook the paratha.
Cook the paratha by flipping over a couple of times until you see golden brown spots on both sides.Proceed the same way with the remaining dough portions and it is ready to be served.Serve the Sweet Potato & Methi Stuffed Paratha Recipe along with Tomato Onion Cucumber Raita , Carrot Cucumber Tomato Salad with Lemon and Coriander to make it a complete meal.
generated_text:
**Diabetic Friendly North Indian Recipe: Sweet Potato & Methi Stuffed Paratha**


**Ingredients:**

- 2 medium-sized sweet potatoes (boiled and mashed)

- 1/2 cup fresh fenugreek leaves (methi)

- 1 teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera)

- 1/2 teaspoon asafoetida (hing)

- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder (haldi)

- 1 teaspoon red chili powder (optional, adjust to taste)

- Salt to taste

- 2 cups whole wheat flour (atta)

- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder

- Oil for cooking


1. In a pan, heat a teaspoon of oil and add cumin seeds. Once they start to crackle, add the methi leaves and sauté for a minute until they turn a little darker in color.

2. Add asafoetida, turmeric powder, and red chili powder (if using) to the methi leaves. Mix well and remove from heat.

3. Add the mashed sweet potatoes to the methi mixture. Add salt to taste and mix thoroughly.

4. In a large bowl, mix the whole wheat flour with baking powder. Make a well in the center and add the sweet potato-methi stuffing.

5. Knead the dough with the stuffing until it's well combined. Make small balls of the dough and roll them out into thin circles, about 6-7 inches in diameter.

6. Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat and place a rolled-out paratha on the pan.

7. Cook for about 2-3 minutes on one side until you see the paratha start to brown.

8. Flip the paratha and brush a little oil on the other side. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the paratha is golden brown and crispy.

reference_text:
**Lu methi paratha recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Potato (Aloo) - boiled and mashed,1/2 tablespoon Red Chilli powder,Water - as required,Ghee - as required,Salt - to taste,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1/2 cup Onion - finely chopped,3 cups Whole Wheat Flour,1/4 tablespoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1/4 tablespoon Garam masala powder,1/2 tablespoon Chaat Masala Powder,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1/2 cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) - chopped,Whole Wheat Flour - for dusting

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Aloo Methi Paratha recipe, in a bowl, mix together wheat flour, salt and oil.Add water little at a time and prepare a soft dough just as we make dough for chapati.
Grease your hands with oil and knead it well.
Cover it and let it rest for some time.Now prepare stuffing the stuffing for the paratha.
Mix mashed potatoes, chopped onions, chopped methi, finely chopped green chillies and fresh coriander properly in a bowl.Now add all dry spiced in mixture including salt, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, chaat masala and mix everything well.Now make even size ball out of paratha dough and roll out the ball in a small circle dusting wheat flour.Add some stuffing in the centre and gather all the corners and seal it well and press it lightly.Dust some wheat flour and roll out the sealed dough into a circle as we do for chapati dough.
Don’t apply too much pressure.Heat a tawa, put the rolled out paratha on it.
Let it cook little bit on one side.Flip it over to the other side and spread some ghee over the paratha which is half cooked.Flip it again, when the second side is half cooked and spread some more ghee.Flip it gain, do it couple of times to get paratha browned and cooked well.Now topped it with butter and serve hot.Serve Aloo Methi Paratha along with Boondi Raita, Bhindi Raita or any other raita of your choice.
generated_text:
Lu Methi Paratha Recipe

Ingredients:

- 2 cups of fresh methi leaves
- 2 cups of all-purpose flour
- 1 teaspoon of salt
- 1 teaspoon of sugar
- 1 teaspoon of carom seeds (ajwain)
- 1 teaspoon of red chili powder
- 1 teaspoon of coriander powder
- 1 teaspoon of cumin powder
- 1 teaspoon of ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tablespoon of oil
- 1 tablespoon of ghee
- Water as needed

Instructions:

1. Wash the methi leaves thoroughly and chop them finely.

2. In a large bowl, mix the all-purpose flour, salt, sugar, carom seeds, red chili powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder.

3. Add the ginger-garlic paste and oil to the flour mixture and mix well.

4. Add water gradually and knead the dough until it becomes soft and pliable.

5. Cover the dough and let it rest for 15 minutes.

6. After the dough has rested, roll it out into small balls.

7. Take one ball of dough and flatten it with your palm.

8. Place a handful of chopped methi leaves in the center of the flattened dough.

9. Brush the edges of the dough with ghee.

10. Roll the dough into a circle, making sure the methi leaves are well distributed.

11. Heat a tawa or griddle on medium heat.

12. Place the rolled paratha on the tawa and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side or until the paratha turns golden brown.

13. Brush the cooked paratha with ghee and serve hot with a side of yogurt or pickle.

Enjoy your delicious Lu Methi Paratha for a healthy and flavorful North Indian breakfast!

reference_text:
**Tandoori Aloo Tikka Recipe - Indian Spiced Roasted Smoked Potatoes**


**Ingredients:**
300 grams Baby Potatoes - boiled & peeled,1/4 cup Hung Curd (Greek Yogurt),1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/4 cup Mint Leaves (Pudina) - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,1 Coal - for giving a smoked flavour,1 tablespoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves),4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,2 tablespoons Gram flour (besan),1 tablespoon Ghee - for cooking,1 inch Ginger - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Tandoori Aloo Tikka Recipe, get all the ingredients prepped and ready.Into a large mixing bowl add all the ingredients for Tandoori Aloo in a bowl except the boiled potatoes and ghee and stir them well until combined.Add the boiled baby potatoes into it and allow it to marinate for 30 minutes.Heat a tablespoon of oil or ghee in a wok or a skillet, add the tandoori aloo mixture.Sauté over high heat for four to five minutes, stirring occasionally and roast the potatoes until all the marination thickens and forms a coat over the aloo.The potatoes/ aloo should be well crusted and golden brown.Once done, check the salt and spice and adjust to suit your taste of the Tandoori Aloo.
Keep this aside in the same pan.
Next step is the give the Tandoori Aloo Tikka a smoked flavour.
Place a large piece of coal on the gas flame and allow it to turn red hot.Place a katori in the center of the tandoori aloo tikka pan, place the red hot coal in the katori/ cup and spoon a little bit of ghee over the coal.
It will begin to emit smoke.
Cover the pan and allow the tandoori aloo tikka to get the smoky flavours.After about 30 seconds, remove the coal cup and the smoked Tandoori Aloo tikka is ready.Serve the Tandoori Aloo Tikka as an appetizer for parties along with Lacha Pyaz & Green chutney
generated_text:
Tandoori Aloo Tikka Recipe - Indian Spiced Roasted Smoked Potatoes


Ingredients:

- 2 large potatoes, peeled and cubed

- 1/2 cup plain yogurt

- 1/4 cup fresh coriander (dhania) leaves, finely chopped

- 1 teaspoon red chili powder (lal mirch)

- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder (haldi)

- 1/2 teaspoon garam masala

- 1/2 teaspoon cumin powder (jeera)

- Salt to taste

- Oil for brushing

- Lemon wedges for serving


1. Marinate the potato cubes in yogurt, coriander leaves, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, cumin powder, and salt. Mix well and let it sit for at least 30 minutes.

5. Serve the tandoori aloo tikka hot with lemon wedges.

reference_text:
**Bendekayi Puli Koddel Recipe - Lady Finger In Coconut Tamarind Curry**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,4 cloves Garlic,2 teaspoons Sunflower Oil,200 grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) - cut into 1 inch,Salt - to taste,2 teaspoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds,30 grams Tamarind Water,1 teaspoon Cumin (Jeera) seeds,2 teaspoons Rice,1/2 cup Fresh coconut - grated,1/2 cup Water,2 tablespoons Jaggery,6 Dry Red Chillies,2 tablespoons Sesame seeds (Til seeds),1/4 teaspoon SSP Asafoetida (Hing),2 sprig Curry leaves,2 Dry Red Chillies,1/2 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Bendekayi Puli Koddel Recipe, into a preheated pan, add oil and okra or Bendekayi.
Sprinkle salt to taste and stir well.
Cover and cook the okras till soft and roasted.
Stir occasionally to ensure even cooking of the Okra.Now we will roast the masalas for the Bendekayi Puli Koddel.Into a preheated pan, add fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, sesame seeds, rice grains, dry red chillies, garlic and stir for 3 to 4 minutes until well roasted.Once done, add the coconut and roast for another 2 minutes.
Turn off the heat after the coconut is light golden in color and allow it to cool.
Once the coconut cools down, add the roasted masala to a mixer grinder along with tamarind, jaggery, water and blend to make a smooth paste and keep it aside.Add the ground masala into the pan with the roasted lady's finger.
Add about 1/2 cup of water, salt to taste and bring the Bendekayi Puli Koddel to a brisk boil.
Boil for 4 to 5 minutes until the curry has thickened and reached the desired consistency.
Once done, switch off the gas.*For the seasoning:Heat oil in a tadka pan.
Add mustard seeds and allow it to crackle.
Once the mustard seeds have crackled, add asafoetida, dry red chillies and curry leaves.
Turn off the heat after few seconds and pour the seasoning over the Bendekayi Puli Koddel.
Give it a mix.Transfer the Bendekayi Puli Koddel into a serving bowl and serve.Serve the Bendekayi Puli Koddel Recipe along with Steamed Rice, Mangalorean Padengi Gassi and Elai Vadam for a comforting lunch.

**Bendekayi Puli Koddel Recipe (Lady Finger in Coconut Tamarind Curry)**


**Ingredients:**

- 500g of fresh lady fingers (Bendekayi)

- 1 cup of grated coconut

- 1/2 cup of tamarind pulp

- 1 tablespoon of oil

- 1 teaspoon of mustard seeds

- 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds

- 1 dried red chili

- 1/2 teaspoon of asafoetida (optional)

- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of red chili powder

- Salt to taste

- 4 cups of water

- Fresh coriander leaves for garnish


1. Wash the lady fingers thoroughly and cut them into 2-inch pieces.

4. Add the tamarind pulp, turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt. Mix well to ensure all the lady fingers are coated.

5. Pour in the water and bring the curry to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for about 10 minutes, or until the lady fingers are tender and the curry has thickened slightly.

6. In a separate pan, dry roast the grated coconut on low heat until it turns golden brown. Let it cool and then grind it to a fine paste.

7. Add the coconut paste to the curry and simmer for another 5 minutes.

8. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves before serving.


Serve this Mangalorean side dish with rice or roti for a delightful vegetarian meal.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Oriya Recipes dessert recipe for Chenna Poda Recipe
reference_text:
**Chenna Poda Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
4 tablespoons Milk,2-3 tablespoons Sugar,1-1/2 tablespoons Ghee,2 tablespoons Sooji (Semolina/ Rava),Cashew nuts - few broken,4 tablespoons Sugar - powdered,250 grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese),1/2 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),Raisins - as required,1 tablespoon Ghee

**Instructions:**
To begin making Chenna Poda, firstly take fresh paneer or homemade chenna and mash it well with your hands.Add powdered sugar and cardamom powder and mix all together properly.Add sooji/rawa, ghee and milk and mix it properly till everything incorporates well.In a separate pan fry the broken pieces of cashews and few raisins and add it to the chenna mix as well.In a baking pan or a tin, add 1 tablespoon of ghee and spread all over the base.Add sugar and hold the pan or baking tray firmly on the gas top and heat the pan base till the sugar starts to caramelize.Turn off the gas and transfer the chenna mix over the caramelized sugar evenly.Tap the pan to settle the mix well and bake the chenna in a pre-heated oven at 180 degree C for 40-45 mins or till the toothpick inserted comes out clean.Take out the baked chenna poda and cool it on a wire wrack for 2 hours approximately.Hold the pan upside down on a serving tray and remove the chenna poda carefully.Cut into slices and serve!Serve Chenna Poda with Masala Chai and Whole Moong Dal Vada.
generated_text:
Chenna Poda, also known as Chhena Poda, is a popular Indian dessert from the Odia cuisine. It's a rich, creamy, and delicious treat that's perfect for any occasion. Here's a simple recipe for making Chenna Poda at home:

Ingredients:

For the Chhena:
- 1 liter of milk
- 1/2 cup of yogurt
- 1/2 teaspoon of citric acid or lemon juice

For the Poda:
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1/2 cup of water
- 1/2 teaspoon of cardamom powder
- 1/2 teaspoon of nutmeg powder
- 1/2 teaspoon of rose water
- 1/2 cup of chopped nuts (almonds, cashews, or pistachios)

Instructions:

1. To make the chhena, heat the milk in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Stir occasionally to prevent the milk from sticking to the bottom.

2. Once the milk starts to boil, add the citric acid or lemon juice and stir well. The milk will start to curdle, and you'll see small lumps forming.

3. Remove the pan from the heat and let the curdled milk sit for a few minutes. The chhena will separate from the whey.

4. Strain the chhena through a cheesecloth or a fine sieve to remove any remaining whey. Squeeze out as much moisture as possible.

5. Transfer the chhena to a bowl and knead it with your hands until it becomes smooth and elastic. Cover it with a clean cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.

6. In the meantime, make the poda. In a small saucepan, combine the sugar, water, cardamom powder, and nutmeg powder. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat.

7. Reduce the heat to low and simmer the poda for 10-15 minutes, or until the sugar has completely dissolved and the mixture has thick

user_message: Generate a High Protein Non Vegetarian North Indian Recipes appetizer recipe for Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka Recipe - Dhaniya Pudina Chicken Tikka Recipe
reference_text:
**Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka Recipe - Dhaniya Pudina Chicken Tikka Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
oil - as per use, 2 tsp lemon juice, 4 cloves garlic, 3 sprig mint, 2 green chillies, 5 sprig green coriander,250 grams chicken - chopped, 2 tbsp Hung curd, 1/2 tsp Ginger - grated, chaat masala powder - as per use, salt - to taste

**Instructions:**
To make Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka recipe, first clean and wash the chicken thoroughly.
Grind it small and make a hole with the help of fork.
Keep it separately.
To make the marination spices, add green coriander, mint, Hung curd, green chillies, ginger, garlic, salt and make a paste in a mixer grinder.
Add chicken to it and mix well.
Keep it in the fridge for 1 hour.
Now heat oil in a grill pan.
Add marinated chicken to it and mix well.
Cook well on all sides.
A light brown layer will start to form on the chicken and the hakiyan will also become soft.
Once cooked, drain in a bowl, add chaat masala, lemon juice and mix well.
Serve coriander mint chicken tikka recipe with yogurt mint chutney and vinegar onion for your house parties.
generated_text:
**Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka Recipe**


Ingredients:

- 500g boneless chicken breast, cut into cubes

- 1/4 cup fresh coriander (dhaniya) leaves, finely chopped

- 1/4 cup fresh mint (pudina) leaves, finely chopped

- 2 tablespoons plain yogurt

- 1 teaspoon lemon juice

- 1 teaspoon garam masala

- 1 teaspoon turmeric powder

- 1 teaspoon cumin powder

- 1 teaspoon paprika

- Salt to taste

- Oil for brushing

- Fresh coriander leaves for garnish


1. In a large bowl, combine the chopped coriander and mint leaves with yogurt, lemon juice, garam masala, turmeric powder, cumin powder, paprika, and salt. Mix well to ensure the spices are evenly distributed.

2. Add the chicken cubes to the marinade and coat them thoroughly. Cover the bowl and let it marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, or preferably overnight, for the flavors to infuse.

3. Preheat your grill or tandoor to a medium-high heat.

4. Once the chicken is marinated, remove it from the refrigerator. Brush the chicken pieces with a little oil and place them on the grill.

5. Grill the chicken for about 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and has a nice char on the outside.

6. Serve the chicken tikka on a platter, garnished with fresh coriander leaves.


Enjoy your high-protein, non-vegetarian North Indian appetizer!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Awadhi one pot dish recipe for Matar Ki Tehri Recipe - Fragrant rice cooked with spices and fresh peas
reference_text:
**Matar Ki Tehri Recipe - Fragrant rice cooked with spices and fresh peas**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Basmati rice,1 Dry Red Chilli,1/4 cup Tomatoes - sliced,1 cup Green peas (Matar),1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,4-5 Cloves (Laung),3 tablespoons Ghee,1/4 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Whole Black Peppercorns,2 Black cardamom (Badi Elaichi),1/4 cup Onions - sliced,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1/4 inch Ginger - finely grated

**Instructions:**
To begin making Matar Ki Tehri, wash and soak the rice in enough water for 1 hour before cooking.
Once it is soaked, drain the water and keep it aside.Soak the peas in a cup of water, so that it cooks faster and retain some of that 'plump' look.Heat ghee in the pressure cooker and add all the whole spices and red chilli and sauté it for 2 minutes on low flame.After 2 minutes, add the sliced onions and ginger and sauté it on low flame for 2-3 minutes till they begin to brown.
Now add the tomatoes and peas and sauté it for another 2 minutes.After 2 minutes, add coriander leaves, bay leaf and the rice.
Add salt according to your taste and mix it well.Add water and cook on high flame till the pressure cooker releases 1 whistle.
Then simmer it down on low flame and let it cook for more 3-4 minutes.Once it is done, turn off the stove and let the cooker release steam and open on its own so that the Tehari is nicely cooked.Serve it hot, either on its own as a one pot meal or else with Lauki Raita, Beetroot Raita or any raita of your choice.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Karnataka snack recipe for Yele Kadubu Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney
reference_text:
**Yele Kadubu Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney**


**Ingredients:**
Water - as required,1 tablespoon Tamarind Paste,Salt - to taste,3 tablespoons Ghee,1-1/2 cups Whole Wheat Flour,2 Red onion,5 tablespoons Jaggery - powdered,1/4 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),10 cloves Garlic,Sunflower Oil - as required,10 Dry Red Chillies - adjustable

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Yele Kadubu Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney, we will firstly make the Yele Kadubu.Mix the wheat flour, salt, and ghee into a mixing bowl.
Mix everything and gradually add water to form a dough.
Once done, keep it aside for 10 minutes.
Take a lemon size dough and roll it like a phulka.
Fold it twice like a triangular shape and make triangles like this for the remaining dough too.Grease an idli steamer and put these Yele Kadubu into it.
Steam the Yele Kadubu for 8 to 10 minutes and they are ready to be served.The next step is to make the chutney for Yele Kadubu.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan.
Add onion, garlic, fenugreek seeds and dry red chillies and cook till the onions become soft and translucent.Switch off the heat and let the onion chutney mixture cool down a bit.
Add this mix into a mixie grinder and add jaggery, salt and tamarind and grind it to smooth paste.Do not add water to this Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney.
Once done, take the Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney out in a bowl and serve with the Yele kadubu.Serve Yele Kadubu  Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney on its own for breakfast or tea time snacks along with Filter Coffee and Masala Chai.

   - Once the seeds start to crackle, add the chopped onions.

   - Cook on a low flame until the onions turn translucent.

   - Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, sugar, and salt.

Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai (Brinjal Stir Fry with Curry Powder)

- 2 tablespoons of besan (gram flour)

- 1 teaspoon of red chili powder

- 1 teaspoon of coriander powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder

- 1/2 teaspoon of asafoetida (hing)

- 1 teaspoon of mustard seeds

- 1 teaspoon of urad dal (split black gram)

- 1 teaspoon of chana dal (split Bengal gram)

- A pinch of hing

generated_text:
Rajasthani Recipe for Lauki Badi Ki Sabzi (Lauki with Chickpea Flour):


Ingredients:

- 1 kg Lauki (Bottle Gourd)

- 1 cup Besan (Chickpea Flour)

- 1/2 cup Ghee (Clarified Butter)

- 1/2 teaspoon Haldi (Turmeric Powder)

- 1/2 teaspoon Jeera (Cumin Seeds)

- 1/2 teaspoon Methi (Fenugreek Seeds)

- 1/2 teaspoon Mustard Seeds

- 1/2 teaspoon Urad Dal (Black Gram)

- 1/2 teaspoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram)

- Salt to taste

- 1/2 cup Water


1. Wash the Lauki thoroughly and cut them into small pieces.

2. Heat the ghee in a pan and add the jeera, methi, mustard seeds, urad dal, and chana dal. Stir them until they start to crackle.

3. Add the chopped Lauki to the pan and sauté for a few minutes.

4. Sprinkle the turmeric powder and salt over the Lauki and mix well.

5. Add the chickpea flour gradually, stirring continuously to avoid lumps.

6. Add water as needed to make a thick gravy.

7. Cook on low flame for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

8. Once the Lauki is cooked and the gravy has thickened, remove from heat.

9. Serve hot with rice or roti.


Enjoy this traditional Rajasthani dish, which is not only delicious but also packed with nutrients.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian lunch recipe for Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe
reference_text:
**Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 cup Neem leaves,1 Dry Red Chilli,Salt - to taste,4 Sweet Potatoes,1 tablespoon Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali) - powdered,1/3 teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split),1 teaspoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1/3 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1.5 tablespoon Sunflower Oil

**Instructions:**
To prepare Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe, clean the neem leaves and tear the stem out of it.Pressure cook the sweet potato for just 1 whistle or 5 minutes whichever is earlier, on full flame.
The pressure will take longer time if you put a lot of water.Take out, peel & chop them into cubes.Take a non-stick pan, add oil, hing, mustard, urad dhal, red chilli & bengal gram dal.
Fry them well.Add the neem leaves and let it fry for 3-4 minutes until they turn slightly crispy.Add the chopped sweet potato and salt.
Cook well until the sweet potato is fried well.Garnish with roasted peanut powder.Serve Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe with hot steaming rasam rice.
generated_text:
**Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe**


Ingredients:

- 2 medium-sized sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed

- 1/2 cup neem leaves, finely chopped (ensure they are clean and free from pesticides)

- 1 large onion, finely chopped

- 2 tomatoes, pureed

- 2 tablespoons of ginger-garlic paste

- 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder

- 1 teaspoon of coriander powder

- 1 teaspoon of cumin powder

- 1 teaspoon of red chili powder (adjust to taste)

- 1/2 teaspoon of asafoetida (optional)

- 1 tablespoon of oil

- Salt to taste

- Fresh coriander leaves for garnish

- Lemon wedges for serving


1. Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the asafoetida and let it sizzle for a few seconds.

2. Add the finely chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent.

3. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until the raw smell dissipates.

4. Add the pureed tomatoes to the pan and cook until the oil starts to separate from the curry mixture.

5. Now, add the turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and red chili powder. Mix well.

6. Add the cubed sweet potatoes and chopped neem leaves to the pan. Stir gently to combine.

7. Cover the pan and let the curry simmer on low heat for about 20 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are cooked through and tender.

8. Season with salt to taste.

9. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot with lemon wedges on the side.


Enjoy this nutritious and flavorful vegetarian curry that's perfect for a healthy lunch!

reference_text:
**Indian Style Kala Chana Salad Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - as required,1 Tomato - finely chopped,2 cups Kala Chana (Brown Chickpeas) - soaked overnight and boiled,1/2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped (adjustable),1 Lemon juice,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves -

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Indian Style Black Chickpea Salad recipe, wash and soak the chana in water,and leave it overnight.

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Baingan Sarson Ka Saag Recipe
reference_text:
**Baingan Sarson Ka Saag Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - sliced,1 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder),1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),3 tablespoons Mustard oil - for cooking,200 grams Mustard greens,1 inch Ginger - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),300 grams Small Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant) - quartered

**Instructions:**
To begin making Baingan Sarson Ka Saag Recipe first prep all the ingredients and keep aside.
Quarter the brinjal and keep them in salted water till you use them.
Keeping them in salted water prevents discolouration of the brinjal.Wash and chop the mustard greens ( sarson ka saag) roughly and keep them aside.
Next heat oil in a deep pan or wok and once the oil has reached a smoking point reduce the heat to medium.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Then add onions, ginger and garlic and saute till the onions are cooked.Now add the drained and cut brinjal slices to the pan and saute.
Sprinkle some water, cover and cook the brinjal till its cooked through and is well roasted.This will take about 4 to 5 minutes.Once the brinjal is well roasted, softened and cooked through, add all the dry spices to the pan, chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder, amchur powder, salt and saute for a minute till all the brinjal is well coated with the masalas.
Finally add the chopped mustard leaves to the brinjals and cover and cook till the mustard greens are cooked through.
This will take about 3 to 4 minutes as well.Check the salt and seasonings of the Baingan Sarson Ka Saag and adjust according to taste.Once done, turn off the heat and transfer the Baingan Sarson Ka Saag to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Baingan Sarson Ka Saag with Phulka , Panchmel Dal and Lauki Raita for a lovely weekday lunch.

7. Add the chopped fenugreek leaves and water. Cook for another 5 minutes.

8. Add the roasted eggplants and cook for 5 minutes

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe - Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry
reference_text:
**Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe - Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,300 grams Elephant yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu) - peeled and diced,1 tablespoon Gram flour (besan),Sunflower Oil - for cooking,1/2 teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns - crushed,1/2 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,1/4 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/2 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder

**Instructions:**
We begin making the Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe (Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry Recipe) by cooking the diced yam in the pressure cooker with 1/2 cup water and a little salt, pressure cook it for 4 whistles till it is soft and firm.
Let the pressure release naturally by itself.The next step is to make the Kadhi so for that add curd in a saucepan and then add 2 cups water and besan flour.
Keep mixing it on a low heat and bring it to a boil.
Add some salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander seeds, stir well and let it cook in low heat for few more minutes.
Later add in the boiled yam to the kadhi.
Once that is done, it is time to make the temper for the kadhi.
Heat a small shallow pan with oil and then add cumin seeds, cinnamon, bay leaf , fenugreek seeds and fresh ground peppercorns.Let the spices get roasted little and sprinkle the tempered spices on to the kadhi mixture.
Serve the Dahi Suran Ki Sabzi Recipe (Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry Recipe) with Bhindi Masala, Jeera Rice and Phulka for an everyday meal.
generated_text:
Ingredients:

- 2 large yams or sweet potatoes
- 1/2 cup plain yogurt
- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
- 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder
- 1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
- 1/2 teaspoon coriander powder
- 1/2 teaspoon garam masala
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon asafoetida (optional)
- 1/4 cup chopped coriander leaves
- 1 tablespoon ghee or oil
- 1/2 cup water

Instructions:

1. Peel and chop the yams into small pieces.

2. In a pressure cooker, add the yams and enough water to cover them. Cook for 5-7 whistles until the yams are soft.

3. In a bowl, mix the yogurt with turmeric, red chili, cumin, coriander, garam masala, salt, and asafoetida. Stir well to combine.

4. Heat the ghee or oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the chopped coriander leaves and sauté for a few seconds.

5. Add the cooked yams to the pan with the sautéed coriander leaves.

6. Pour the yogurt mixture over the yams and stir well to combine.

7. Add water to the pan and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.

8. Once the mixture has thickened, remove from heat and serve hot with rice or roti.

Enjoy your delicious North Indian Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe!

reference_text:
**Capsicum Masala Poriyal Recipe (South Indian Style Bell Pepper Stir Fry)**


**Ingredients:**
4 Dry Red Chillies,Salt - to taste,Curry leaves - Few,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),4 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,3 Garlic - cloves,3 Red Yellow or Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) - diced into cubes,1 tablespoon Fresh coconut,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1 Onion - diced into cubes,1/2 cup Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Capsicum Masala Poriyal Recipe place a kadai on the heat.
Add some oil and warm it.Next, add in the mustard seeds and allow them to splutter.
Next, add the chopped onions and saute them until translucent.Next, toss in the capsicum, curry leaves, required salt and stir-fry for 5 minutes.In the mean time add all the ingredients under “For the Powder” in a blender and grind to a coarse powder.Add in the ground powder to the capsicum mixture and saute for 5 minutes until the capsicum is coated well with the spices.The capsicum pieces should have a slight crunch.
Do not overcook them.Turn off the heat and garnish with coriander.Serve hot with Egg Masoor Dal and Chapatis.

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe - Sweetened With Jaggery
reference_text:
**Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe - Sweetened With Jaggery**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoons Pistachios - slivered,1/2 cup Jaggery - powdered,1-1/4 cups Lotus Seeds,3 tablespoons Badam (Almond) - slivered,1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 tablespoon Ghee,2 teaspoons Dried rose petals - for garnish,1 liter Milk

**Instructions:**
To begin making Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe, we will firstly roast the makhanas.Heat ghee in a heavy bottomed pan, add the makhanas and slow roast it till it turns golden and crisp.
Keep it aside.Now, save a hand full of this roasted makhanas and pound the rest roughly using mortar and pestle.
Pour milk into a heavy bottomed pan, bring it to a boil on medium heat.
Boil the milk for 10 more minutes, till it becomes a little thicker.Now, add the pounded and whole roasted makhana's and mix well.
Simmer this mixture for 5 minutes, till it becomes creamy and soft.After 5 minutes, add in the powdered jaggery, cardamom powder, mix well and simmer till the jaggery is melted and well incorporated into the kheer.Switch off the heat, garnish with silvered nuts, dried rose petals and the Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe (Sweetened With Jaggery) is ready to serve.Serve the Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe as a part of the Festive Menu this Diwali and let your guests rejoice in the festive spirits.
You can also serve it as a dessert after your everyday meal of Paneer Butter Masala, Kachumber Salad and Tawa Paratha.
generated_text:
Ingredients:

- 1 cup Phool Makhana (Fox Nuts)
- 2 cups Milk
- 1/2 cup Jaggery, finely grated
- 1/2 teaspoon Cardamom Powder
- 1/4 teaspoon Saffron Strands
- 1/4 teaspoon Ghee (Clarified Butter)
- A pinch of Salt

Instructions:

1. Rinse the Phool Makhana (Fox Nuts) thoroughly under cold water. Drain the water and let them dry for a few minutes.

2. In a heavy-bottomed pan, add the Phool Makhana (Fox Nuts) and 1 cup of milk. Cook on low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally until the milk is almost completely absorbed.

3. Add the remaining 1 cup of milk to the pan and continue cooking on low heat for another 10 minutes.

4. Add the finely grated jaggery, cardamom powder, saffron strands, and a pinch of salt to the pan. Stir well to combine all the ingredients.

5. Cook the mixture on low heat for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally until the jaggery is completely dissolved and the mixture has thickened slightly.

6. Remove the pan from the heat and let the Phool Makhana Kheer cool down for a few minutes.

7. Once coo0led, add a teaspoon of ghee to the kheer and mix well.

8. Serve the Phool Makhana Kheer warm or chilled, garnished with a few strands of saffron and a sprinkle of cardamom powder.

Enjoy this delicious and healthy North Indian dessert made with fox nuts and sweetened with jaggery!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Himachal side dish recipe for Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe - Spicy Tangy Yam
reference_text:
**Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe - Spicy Tangy Yam**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1/2 teaspoon Kala jeera,1 inch Ginger - chopped,1/2 teaspoon Kalonji (Onion Nigella Seeds),2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,Mustard oil - for cooking,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 Onion - chopped,300 grams Elephant yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu) - chopped small,1 sprig Curry leaves,1/2 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),4 cloves Garlic - chopped,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe, heat a pressure cooker with oil, add all the seeds that are mentioned above including cumin seeds, kalonji, methi seeds, kala jeera and mustard seeds.Allow it to crackle for few seconds.
Add in curry leaves and leave to splutter for few seconds.Add ginger and garlic and saute until they soften.
Add in onions and cook until they turn golden brown.Add chopped yam along with all the spice powders and salt to taste and give it a stir.Add about 1 cup of water and pressure cook the yam for atleast 3 whistle until it becomes soft.Once done switch off the heat and allow the pressure to release naturally and bring the cooker back on the heat.Open the lid and keep sauteing until the water has all evaporated and the subzi thickens.Serve the Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe along with Kadhi, Bhakri and Cauliflower & Cucumber Raita by the side for a delicious meal.
generated_text:
Ingredients:

- 2 large yams (jimikand)
- 1 tablespoon of oil
- 1 teaspoon of mustard seeds
- 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds
- 1 small red onion, finely chopped
- 1 green chili, finely chopped
- 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder
- 1 teaspoon of red chili powder
- 1 teaspoon of coriander powder
- Salt to taste
- 1/2 cup of water
- Fresh coriander leaves for garnish

Instructions:

1. Wash the yams thoroughly and peel them. Cut them into small, bite-sized pieces.

2. In a large pan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds and cumin seeds, and let them splutter.

3. Add the finely chopped onion and green chili to the pan. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.

4. Add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, and coriander powder to the pan. Mix well to combine the spices with the onion and chili.

5. Add the yam pieces to the pan and mix well to coat them with the spice mixture.

6. Add salt to taste and mix well.

7. Add 1/2 cup of water to the pan and cover it with a lid. Let the yams cook over low heat for 10-15 minutes, or until they are tender and the water has evaporated.

8. Once the yams are cooked, remove the pan from the heat and garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

9. Serve the Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe hot with rice or roti. Enjoy!

reference_text:
**Singhara Pulao Recipe - Water Chestnut Mixed Rice**


**Ingredients:**
Sunflower Oil,2 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,Salt - to taste,2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,2 cups Cooked rice,300 grams Singhade (water chestnut) - pressure cooked and skin peeled,2 Cloves (Laung),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 Star anise

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Singhara Pulao Recipe (Water Chestnut Rice Recipe), keep the cooked rice ready.Pressure cook the water chestnuts with water for at least 8 whistles and allow it to rest for few seconds until it releases pressure naturally.Once done, take the water chestnuts out and peel off the black skin and keep the see aside.
Chop them into half.In a kadai, heat some oil, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cloves and star anise and allow it to sizzle.Add in cooked water chestnuts, salt to taste, turmeric powder and pepper powder.
Saute until they are well combined.

reference_text:
**Bhindi Raita Recipe (Okra Salad)**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 tablespoon Groundnut powder - roasted,1 teaspoon Sugar,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,3/4 cup Hung Curd (Greek Yogurt) - whisked,Salt - to taste,1/2 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),2 tablespoons Sunflower Oil,250 grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) - cleaned,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder

Cool it down.In a mixing bowl add the curd.
Add little salt, sugar, groundnut powder, chaat masala and mix.In a small wok/pan, heat oil.
Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and hing.Add the crisp okras in the curd and pour the tempering on the curd.
The raita is ready to be served.Serve Bhindi Raita with roti or Rice and Panchmel Dal for a light everyday meal.

user_message: Generate a Kerala Recipes recipe for Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe - Kerala Mutton Masala
reference_text:
**Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe - Kerala Mutton Masala**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - as per your taste,1/2 teaspoon Fennel seeds (Saunf),2 sprig Curry leaves,1 Tomato - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,3/4 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,6 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,2 inch Ginger - finely chopped,1/4 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),500 grams Mutton - with bone (adds flavour),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),3 tablespoons Coconut Oil

**Instructions:**
To begin making Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe, wash and clean the mutton pieces well.
In a pressure cooker, combine the mutton pieces, pepper powder, salt, turmeric powder and mix well.Add 1 cup of water.
Close the pressure cooker and place on high flame.Once the first whistle blows, reduce the flame to low and then let it continue to be on low flame for another 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, turn off the heat and allow the pressure to release naturally.
Transfer the mutton pieces in a bowl and keep the stock aside.
The stock will be utilized later.
In a deep pan or wok add coconut oil on medium heat.
Once the oil is hot, add sliced onion and saute for about 3-4 minutes until the onions become soft and translucent.Now add fennel seeds, ginger, garlic and curry leaves and let it cook for 2 minutes.
To the onion mix, add red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder salt and stir well.
Saute for a couple of minutes until the Kerala Mutton Roast masala comes together.Once the raw smell of the masala is gone, add the chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy.
This will take about 2 to 3 minutes.At this stage add the mutton stock and the mutton pieces into this Mutton Roast masala.

reference_text:
**Maharashtrian Style Namkeen Shankarpali (Savory Flaky Pastry Diamonds Recipe)**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoon Sooji (Semolina/ Rava),2 tablespoon Red Chilli powder,4 tablespoon Ghee,2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 cups Whole Wheat Flour,1 teaspoon Salt

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Namkeen Shankarpali, mix the flour, semolina, red chilli powder, salt, cumin seeds in a bowl.Heat the ghee till it is light hot and pour it into the flour mixture.
Carefully mix to form a crumbly texture.
Make sure you do not burn hand.Add little water and knead it into a soft dough, rest the dough covered in a warm place for at least 30 minutes.We will start rolling the dough on a flat surface, sprinkle little flour if you feel the dough is very sticky.
Roll it to about 2 centimeter thick.Using a knife cut the dough into small diamond shapes or any desired shape and arrange them on a tray to fry.Heat a kadai with oil to deep fry the Shankarpalis.
Once the oil is hot carefully drop them and fry till they become light brown.Using a skimmer take the shakarpalis out and place it on the tissue paper in order to drain out the oil.Serve the Namkeen Shankarpali along with hot Masala Chai or South Indian Filter Coffee to make your evening special.

Here is a recipe for Maharashtrian Style Namkeen Shankarpali (Savory Flaky Pastry Diamonds):

- 2 cups all-purpose flour

- 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder
- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
- 1/2 teaspoon garam masala
- 1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
- 1/2 teaspoon coriander powder

- 1/2 teaspoon sugar

- 1/2 teaspoon oil

Instructions:
